t: Miami Amigos steal Spring 2023 28+ Division Crown with 6-5 Walk-Off Win over Aventura Braves
b:
It’s a situation a player can dream on. The bases loaded, no one out in the bottom of the 9th, down three runs, and then suddenly, two outs, full count, runners in scoring position and the championship one swing away either way. Eddie Cabrera would find himself in this situation, and would take full advantage of his opportunities and make his impact known on the base-paths, inserting his name in SFBL championship lore.
The postseason is certainly not without its dramatics and its definitely not without its controversy, those two things are for certain. And it certainly tends to be a back and forth affair. The Miami Amigos (28+ division) and Aventura Braves would follow that baseball narrative to a tee. In the 2nd inning, the Braves were set up beautifully and managed to get a run. The Amigos tied it. The Braves scored two runs in the 5th and took a 3-1 lead. The Amigos came back in the 6th to make it a one run game again. Going into the 7th, the Braves pushed two more runs across to reinstate their previous two run advantage, 5-3.
Cabrera forced an interference call between 2nd and 3rd base in the bottom of the 9th with two outs and runners on 2nd and 3rd down two runs, after Marcell Castaneda, the pitcher for Braves, balked the runners over from 1st and 2nd, and the highest of high pressure moments after Gil Rodriguez hit a hard grounder to 3rd that resulted in a 6-2-3 double play with the score 5-4 Braves and the championship hanging on the line.
While both teams fumed at the bang bang play at 1st in which the umpires ruled Gil out on a force-play, it was superseded by the very next play. In stepped Cabrera, the team’s manager and soon-to-be-MVP. On a full count, he ripped a bullet down the first-base line, just out of the reach of the diving infielder for a single that scored a run to even the score at 5. The ball tipped off the first baseman Alonso George’s glove and ricocheted down the line. Cabrera then stole 2nd, a play contested by the Braves. He was awarded an interference call on the slide. While the Braves argued, not realizing that the play was still live, Cabrera, the ultimate IQ player, never stopped running and took third. Jose Aguada came through with a hard grounder towards the shortstop hole and the ball took a bad hop, squirted in between center and left field and scored Cabrera. The Braves shortstop, Alex Roa, threw a seed to home plate. But Cabrera, correctly calculating the angle of the throw, swam around the tag and scored with a hand-first tag for a 6-5 lead — after trailing all game long. The MVP was awarded to Cabrera who went 3-5 with two runs batted in, and two stolen bases, including the one that won the game.
Other key performers included right-handed pitcher Ruben Ramos who went 7 1/3rd innings pitched. He struck out six and surrendered seven hits, Eric Whitehurst who could’ve very well been granted MVP with his own 3-4 day, and both Gus Castillo and Gil Rodriguez who went 2-4.
“It was a good game. We’ve always have good competition against them. They beat us in the regular season. It was kind of a heated exchange. I was injured for that regular season matchup. I wanted the revenge. We’ve knocked them out of the playoffs 3 out of the last 4 seasons. Our games with them always come down to the bottom of the 9th, and controversy is typically how these games ends. They have not been able to beat us in the playoffs. We look forward to our future games with those guys, and beating them is the best feeling in the world,” said the MVP and manager Eddie Cabrera.

Fall 2013 Season ended with three deserving Champions.
PREMIER DIVISION - Coral Gables Athletics sweep the Miami Dodgers to claim 10th title
The Coral Gables Athletics won their 10th title, staking claim to being the best team ever. They blanked the Miami Dodgers 15-0 in game 1 and completed the sweep 7-1 in game 2. To reach the finals the A's survived the Miami Reds 5-4, while the Miami Dodgers shocked the Miami Marlins by scoring two runs in the bottom of the ninth to walk off 5-4.
SENIOR DIVISION - Miami Blues beat South Florida Beach Bums 2 games to 1 for third title in a row (BLUES THREEPEAT)
The Miami Blues won the Fall Senior Division Championship for the 3rd year in a row. Will Dorsey and Michael Jordan dueled on the mound. Ryan O'Malley hit a 2-strike home run over the left-center wall, Sean Emory tripled, and Will Dorsey hit a monster home run for a 4-0 lead. In the 4th, singles by Chris Antonelli, Brent Lozano and Ray Tolmos loaded the bases; Sean Emory walked in a run and Will Dorsey hit a grand slam (his 2nd HR, 6th RBI) for a 9-1 lead. The Blues cruised to a 12-1 championship win. Dorsey's line: 1 run, 5 hits, 0 BB, 11 K. To reach the series the Blues beat the Miami Brewers 12-8, while the Beach Bums shocked the Perrine Monarchs 5-4 on a bottom-of-the-ninth walk-off.
MASTERS DIVISION - Dade Tigers 8, Palm Beach Angels 4 - new team wins it all (CHAMPIONS!!!)
A pitching combination of Hernandez/Paylor, clutch hitting and stellar defense powered the Dade Tigers past the undefeated Palm Beach Angels of Coconut Creek. The Angels led 1-0 after one. Eddy Castellanos' single up the middle scored Duane Medina and Ramon Poo for a 2-1 lead; Angel Estevez's single made it 3-1. Alain Hernandez strained his groin in the 4th and could not continue as the Angels tied it 3-3. The Tigers went ahead 4-3 in the 6th on Estevez's double and Garvi Arias' double. In the 8th, Jorge Castellanos cleared the bases with a 0-2 triple for a 7-3 lead; the Tigers added one in the 9th to win 8-3 (final 8-4). Co-MVPs: Jorge Castellanos (2-3, 3B, 3 RBI, SB, SAC, 8 defensive plays) and Ryan Paylor (win in relief, 5 1/3 IP, 7 K, 2 BB, 4 H, 1 R). Over 18 postseason innings Hernandez and Paylor combined for 18 K, 5 BB, 5 R (3 ER), 10 H. Team MVP: catcher Danger Villalobos.
Angels Fall To Tigers In Championship Tilt (by Palm Beach Angels): For the second season in a row the Palm Beach Angels of Coconut Creek fell one game short of the Masters crown, dropping an 8-4 decision. To reach the finals the Angels beat the Miramar Rangers 11-0 behind Alvaro Gomez's complete-game one-hitter (9 K), with Anthony DeFilippis, Keith Nicoll, Paul Tunink and Phil Laufman collecting two hits each. Meanwhile the Dade Tigers beat the Broward Black Sox 4-1, holding the defending champions to 2 hits and 1 unearned run behind Hernandez and Paylor.

2012 marked The South Florida Baseball League's 20 year anniversary and 40th consecutive season of operation. Fall 2012 was a very exciting season.
PREMIER DIVISION
The Premier Division's playoff race came down to the wire, with The Miami Marlins, Miami Hurricanes, and Aventura Braves all fighting for the 3rd and 4th playoff positions. In the end, The Marlins and Hurricanes edged out The Braves. The Boca Red Sox clinched the 1st position, going undefeated in the regular season. The Miami Dodgers in their 1st season in the SFBL finished a solid 2nd place.
Final standings (Team, W-L-T, G, Pct, P):
Boca Red Sox 12-0-0, 12, 1.000, 24
Miami Dodgers 8-4-0, 12, .667, 16
Miami Marlins 6-5-1, 12, .542, 13
Miami Hurricanes 6-6-0, 12, .500, 12
Aventura Braves 5-6-1, 12, .458, 11
Miami Jays 4-7-1, 12, .375, 9
Miami Brewers 4-7-1, 12, .375, 9
Kendall Metz 1-11-0, 12, .083, 2
Playoffs: The Red Sox eliminated the Hurricanes 11-5. The Marlins beat the Dodgers 5-1. In the championship series the Marlins swept the Red Sox 3-1 and 9-2. The Marlins repeated as champions.
SENIOR DIVISION
The Miami Blues went 11-1, their only loss to the Miami Brewers in the Premier Division. The Davie Tigers went 9-2-1 for 2nd. The final two playoff spots were contested by the Bay Stars, Black Bats, Southern Yankees and Dade Diamondbacks; the Black Bats and Diamondbacks secured them.
Final standings (Team, W-L-T, G, Pct, P):
Miami Blues 11-1-0, 12, .917, 22
Davie Tigers 9-2-1, 12, .792, 19
Dade Diamondbacks 8-4-0, 12, .667, 16
Miami Black Bats 7-4-1, 12, .625, 15
Miami BayStars 7-5-0, 12, .583, 14
Southern Yankees 5-7-0, 12, .417, 10
South Florida Rays 3-9-0, 12, .250, 6
Miami Red Sox 3-9-0, 12, .250, 6
Deerfield White Sox 0-12-0, 12, .000, 0
Playoffs: The Blues beat the Black Bats 5-1 in a 2-hour-5-minute pitcher's duel. The Diamondbacks beat the Tigers 4-1. In the championship the Blues beat the Diamondbacks 9-1 (Diamondbacks left 16 on base). The Blues won their 2nd straight championship.
MASTERS DIVISION
The Hollywood Twins went 10-2. Four teams battled for the other three playoff spots - the Florida Yankees, Broward Black Sox, Dade Nationals and Miramar Rangers. The Yankees lost to the Nationals 9-3 and tied the Rangers 2-2 to miss the playoffs by the smallest margin.
Final standings (Team, W-L-T, G, Pct, P):
Hollywood Twins 10-2-0, 12, .833, 20
Dade Nationals 7-5-0, 12, .583, 14
Miramar Rangers 6-5-1, 12, .542, 13
Broward Black Sox 6-6-0, 12, .500, 12
Florida Yankees 5-6-1, 12, .458, 11
South Florida Dodgers 1-11-0, 12, .083, 2
Playoffs: The Twins beat the Black Sox 8-5 behind a complete game by Bob Westheimer. The Rangers beat the Nationals 9-8. In the championship the Rangers defeated the Twins 10-4 to win their 2nd title in a row.

t: Dade Nationals blow out Southern Yankees 13-5, Repeat 35+ Division Champs Spring 2023 Season
b:
Dade Nationals Demolish Southern Yankees; Crowned Champs For 9th Straight Time.
The Southern Yankees certainly didn't see this coming after a stellar 10-2 regular season. They were so offensively mighty that they mercy-ruled teams five times during the regular season. Unfortunately for the Yankees, they were facing a franchise in the Dade Nationals, a group that is accustomed to winning championships. They had previously won an unprecedented eight championships in a row, this last of these by a score of 13-5. Their intensity right out of the gate kept the Yankees reeling.
The Yankees didn't hit at all against the tough flame throwing lefty. Mike Ennis and Melvyn Martinez had two hits apiece for the Yankees in a very quiet offensive display. Ray Asmar, the manager of the Yankees noted, "The big offensive production, usually a staple this season, just wasn't there from our talented players. We have lots to be thankful and proud of. Throughout the season we played short-handed sometimes with 9 or 10 players but still managed to have a stellar record. We had lots of fun in the dugout with all the good natured ribbing and laughs. Many players had a breakout season but somehow we managed to self-destruct defensively and didn't hit like we normally did, but we still have lots to be proud of as a team."
The Nationals were led by Ramon Alfonso, who is familiar with the big moment. He's previously won MVP's of championship games, most recently in the Fall 2022 season. Alfonso stymied the Yankees, hurling a complete game. He had a Ruthian performance, sparkling both on the mound and beside the plate. He racked up four hits including a pair of doubles, one of them a bases clearing double that put the game away early in the 3rd inning. The Yankees turned in a sloppy defensive performance, committing three errors in total, and the Nationals proved ruthless opportunists. They ultimately turned those mistakes into three extra runs as the previous champs were re-crowned for the ninth time. "It's no easy feat, winning 9 championships in a row, but this club is so together. We're looking forward to winning as many as possible together. We want to keep adding to the trophy case," said Nationals manager Jorge Orbeta.

t: Margate Marlins take Spring 2023 18+ Division Title with fierce 2-1 Win over Team Venezuela
b:
Margate Marlins vs Team Venezuela 18+ Open Division Championship Game
Managed by Kelvin Abreu, the Margate Marlins entered the spring 2023 postseason as the 3rd seed, but that was trouble enough for the rest of the playoff teams.
Marlins starter righty Ariel Burgos was dueling through seven innings. He scattered just six hits and six k’s, with only one free pass allowed, and only one earned run. Despite that, he was matching up against former Phillies prospect, now starter for Team Venezuela, left hander Orestes Meléndez, who was on top of his game. In a pitchers duel, Meléndez went all nine innings, the first time he had done so according to his manager, Venezuela’s coach Hector Garcia, and struck out a career high 16 batters. Despite his “Outstanding, amazing, unheralded effort,” the Marlins had a counter.
Fireballing closer Miguel Castellanos entered in the top of the 8th inning, and changed the dynamic of the one-all game. He ripped an RBI double, later scoring himself as the Marlins rallied in the 8th for the two runs, and then blew away Venezuela with two innings of four strikeout ball that would end up winning them the championship, a true two way MVP performance both at the bat and on the mound.
“In our quarterfinals win against the Braves we were down 4-0, and we scored 12 unanswered and beat them 12-4. In the semi-finals beat the Reds 1-0, and then our pitching won us the finals, the championship game 2-1,” Marlins’ manager Kelvin Abreu reflected. “That’s the mark of a good team. It was an emotional game, about responding. The thing about our team is to us it’s not just a baseball team. A lot of us don’t have family here, so we’re a family. A group of guys who are brothers. We’ve been playing together for 7-8 seasons just having fun and playing ball. We play our game, and,” the skipper added, “We’re going to defend our title.”
Ariel Burgos (#25) and Miguel Castellanos (#14) were awarded co-MVPs.

t: MIAMI JC 18+ WALKS OFF SERINCA BASEBALL 2-1 IN CHAMPIONSHIP OF ’22 FALL SEASON
b:
2	Featured
3	News
4	MIAMI JC 18+ WALKS OFF SERINCA BASEBALL 2-1 IN CHAMPIONSHIP OF ’22 FALL SEASON
Miami JC Victorious In Walk-Off Clincher In 2-1 Championship ‘W’ over Serinca Baseball Club.
The formerly known Serinca Baseball Club fell on the final play of the Fall ’22 season to Miami JC 18+ 2-1. Serinca’s Ruben Sanchez took a very tough Nathan Eovaldi type game three of the 2018 World Series loss vs the Dodgers, vs Miami JC. The starter frankly dominated, tossing 9 1/3rd innings, giving up only two runs on eight hits, striking out eight and walking only three, and took a tough luck loss in the championship game. On the other side, lefty starter Rodolfo Mederos, ultimately named MVP of the game, matched Sanchez through 7 brilliant innings, surrendering only one hit, and one run, earning the win and the title for Miami JC. Their pitchers struck out 14 Serinca hitters. The game was tied at one in the seventh inning after Juan Carlos Llerena took the seventh pitch of the at bat from Ruben Sanchez and lined a RBI single to center. In the bottom of the 10th when Rodnney Rodriguez singled on a fly ball to the warning track in left field, scoring ghost runner Yasmani Prieto from 2nd with the go-ahead run and ultimately the winning run. The finale of the game was not without its controversy. That single hit the Serinca outfielder David Gonzalez’s glove square in the pocket of the glove, and bounced in and out of his control despite it looking as if he caught or trapped the ball. The play could have resulted in either an out or an error, or ultimately the single it was ruled to be by the umpires. Serinca fumed as Miami JC celebrated their Fall 2022 championship.

t: ALL STAR GAME – a thriller on the beach!
b:
The South Florida Baseball League hosted the 2015 All Star Game on July 5, 2015 at the beautiful Flamingo Park on Miami Beach. Led by manager Dave Carnevale from the Davie Tigers, the American League Team beat the George Kenhan’s National League Team 11-7 in a come-from-behind effort. Last year’s All Star MVP Jay Warman, South Florida Sting Rays, recorded the biggest blast of the day with a 7th inning game-tying bomb to left field but teammate, Nestor Estevez, from the Broward Indians won this year’s All Star MVP with a performance that included going 2-3, with a double, triple, 3 RBI and clutch defense. Bobby Vega, Miami Brewers, and Alex Catala, South Florida Dodgers, each went 3-3 to lead the National Team in offense.
American League roster: Dave Carnevale (mgr); Broward Indians – Alex Dejesus, Nestor Estevez; Davie Tigers – Bory Rios, Cheyenne Hernandez; Broward White Sox – David Marquez, Mike Stacker; South Florida Beach Bums – Bo Diaz, Ralph Soto; South Florida Sting Rays – Jay Warman, Adrian Roznowski; Miami Lakes Trojans – Joel Colon, Jesus Rivero; Palm Beach Angels/Miramar Rangers; Southern Yankees – Frank Crupi; Boca Giants – Sam Rodney, Daniel McNutt.
National League roster: George Kenhan (mgr); Miami Brewers – Bobby Vega; Miami Diamondbacks – Yanny Valdez, Alex Nate; Miami Dodgers – Sandro Gordillo, Luis Sabatella; Miami Reds – Bo Mulford, Tony Suco; Miami Red Sox – Eli Montag, Javy Rosario; Westchester Orioles – Andy Celaya, Emilio Domingue; Florida Yankees – Ruben Dario, Eduardo Fernandez; Dade Nationals – Angel Correa; South Florida Dodgers – Alex Catala, Carlos Pandolfo.

Premier Division Champions – Miami Marlins
The Miami Marlins swept the series against a tough Boca Red Sox team. Dominate pitching performances by Nick Renault in games 1 and 2 helped lead the Marlins to victory over the Red Sox. Nick pitched a complete game in the series opener as the Marlins edged the Red Sox 2-1. Nick hurled another 6 innings in game 2 in a series clincher that only lasted 7 innings due to the 10 run knockout rule. The Marlins's offensive attack was lead by Nick Silva and David Monteagudo as the Marlins won 13-2 to go on and capture the Premier Division Championship.
Senior Division Champions – Aventura Braves
The Aventura Braves cap their perfect season by going 12-0 and beating the Miami Black Bats 4-0 to win their first Senior Division Championship.
Masters Division Champions – Miramar Rangers
The Broward Black Sox and Miramar Rangers put on an offensive clinic at the Masters Division Championship game. Starting pitcher Albert Gonzalez had to leave the game early with an injury, but Rick Cabello took the mound and lead the Rangers to victory with a strong pitching performance. Rick also provided a spark at the plate where he collected a single, homerun and drove in 3 runs. The Rangers's hitters came alive against a tough Black Sox team as Tony Demarco and Tony Sanchez each had multi-hit games. Fernando Urtiaga and Albert Gonzalez both homered as the Rangers outslug the Broward Black Sox 17-13 to take the Masters Division Championship.

t: Miami Brewers -Spring 2015 Season Open Division Champions
b:
Congratulations to the Miami Brewers – our REPEAT Open Division Champs!
Here is the Brewers first hand game summary:
The Brewers matched again for the Championship with the Davie Tigers for the 2nd straight season. Sean Hernandez went the distance allowing 1 run on 2 hits with 7 K's. The momentum keeper and 1st hightlight of the game came in the bottom of the 4th inning with 2 outs and runner on 3rd as George K singled up the middle for the 1st run of the game. Popeye Hernandez Sean's father followed with a monster shot over the Right fielders head, George K looked at the Right fielder turn his back to him and never looked back again with a head 1st slide into home plate for the 2nd run. That boosted the Brewers confident for the game and followed with a great defensive play in the top of the 6th inning when the Tigers were trying to get back in the game down 3-0. Men on 2nd & 3rd with no outs and infield half way in, grounder to Short Joey Kenhan threw a perfect strike to Popeye at home plate for the tag followed with a throwback to the pitcher Sean who saw the 1st base runner looking down and off the bag. Sean immediately made eye contact with Bengi Seda at 1st base threw and tag the runner out before he got back. That was the dagger to the Heart of the Tiger. Hats off to the Tigers on a great game played. Congratulations to the Brewers on their Back to back championships and 5th overall.

t: Dacar Senators Win Fall '22 28+ Title 10-2 Over Amigos
b:
Dacar Senators Defeat Miami Amigos to Clinch Fall 2022 South Florida Baseball League 28+ Title.
The championship trophy was hoisted by the Dacar Senators (7-6) over the Miami Amigos at Flamingo Park in Miami on July 16th, 2023. In the 28+ division championship matchup between the two SFBL ball-clubs, the final score was 10-2. The Amigos were favored going into the game with a 10-2 record. Managed by Hernan Mejias, the Dacar club responded brilliantly. Robert Palencia, the starter tapped by Mejias for the title game, was simply dominant. The righty starter pitched a complete game, allowing just one earned run over 9 full innings, easily being the MVP of the game for Dacar. "Our bats were in good form that day, and we took full advantage of every opportunity. We ran with confidence. We stole some bases, and every time we had guys on base we drove them in," said the manager Hernan Mejias. "That's the mark of a good team, that you feel like everybody 1-9 is gonna get you home."
"Sometimes you win games you weren't expecting to win. Give all the credit to those guys over there," said Amigos manager Eddie Cabrera. "They played a hell of a game. They were more thoroughly prepared and they had a great game out there. Sometimes, you gotta tip your cap and say good game, boys. You got us. But we'll get you next time."

t: Palm Beach Angels Take Senior Division Crown – Declared Winningest Team this side of the Mississippi!
b:
PALM BEACH ANGELS – SENIOR DIVISION CHAMPS!
The Palm Beach Angels , once again, proved why they are one of the best teams EVER to dominate the SFBL SENIOR Division, claiming yet another title with a 6-4 win over a powerhouse Miami Mariners team in the SENIOR DIVISION CHAMPIONSHIP Sunday, June 23, 2019.
The Mariners struck early and often, building a 4-0 lead, only to watch the Angels tie it up in the sixth. Angels Ace, Matt Leban , then shut down the Mariners the rest of the game and Alvaro Gomez hit the go-ahead SAC fly in the eighth and, just like that, the Angels did it again, winning their umpteenth title (researchers are still adding up their wins). Matt Leban also picked up another championship victory to add to his portfolio of BIG GAME WINS.
Angels skipper, Jamie Siragusa , announced the entire Angels team won the MVP honors and Jamie himself now has a trophy shop at home to house all the League hardware he has collected over the years.
Watch out for the Miami Mariners – the team is for real, having dominated the Senior Division this past season and coming just so close to winning it all. Look for Jeremiah Schnapp and the Miami Mariners to be in the finals again this coming season.
Jamie Siragusa and the Palm Beach Angels – the winningest team this side of the Mississippi!

The Fall 2023 Season produced three new division champions for the first time in years.
In the 18+Division, Miami JC Baseball Club won another title with a 9-0 win over the no show JEG Reds team, taking the title away from the first-time champs Margate Marlins from the prior season.  In the 28+Division, the Miami Amigos also could not repeat as they fell to the Broward Senators 2-0, in a very tough championship contest.  The Senators have now won five titles over the years.  In the 35+ Division, the Broward Marlins emerged as the best team around winning their first title with a solid 15-12 win over the luckless Southern Yankees, who continue to be the eternal bridesmaids after losing another heartbreaker championship. The Dade Nationals had won eight straight titles before the Marlins took care of business.
Each team won the $1,000 cash prize along with championship trophies, t-shirts and authentic SFBL logo baseball caps.
Broward Marlins below:
Broward Senators below:
Miami JC Baseball Club below:

t: Ft Lauderdale Pilots Take MASTERS 40 PLUS Title with 11-0 Win vs Miami Cardinals
b:
Ft Lauderdale Pilots – one word – UNDEFEATED!
The Fort Lauderdale Pilots won the Spring 2019 MASTERS 40 PLUS DIVISION Championship with an 11-0 win over the surprising Miami Cardinals, Sunday November 24, 2019 at Northeast High Baseball Park. Mike Fields was named Most Valuable Player and team manager, Jamie Siragusa, collected another trophy to add to his substantial collection of hardware.
To document the incredible run posted by the Fort Lauderdale Pilots and the Ft. Lauderdale Angels, when the team played in the Senior 30 PLUS Division, we offer the following team history – are you ready? OK here goes…..9 championships with a cumulative record of 166-7-3 all time. WOW!! This includes 107 wins out of the last 108 games, including an 80 game winning streak and a current 27 game winning streak. We are all speechless.
Photo captions: Mike Fields (MVP); Mike Fields with Jamie Siragusa; Alvaro Gomez, doing what he does best – pounding the crap out of baseballs! Pilots skipper Jamie Siragusa legs out a base hit in the eighth inning. Miami Cardinals – Spring Season Masters 40 Plus Division Finalists. Miami Cardinals ace Ted Gilhuly.

South Florida Baseball League Fall 2025 Season champions:
18+ Division: Miami JC Baseball (Champion) def. Boynton Black Sox (Runner-Up), 7-3
28+ Division: Broward Senators (Champion) def. Miami Amigos (Runner-Up), 7-2
35+ Division: Miami Yankees (Champion) def. Dade Nationals (Runner-Up), 6-3
The Fall 2025 Season produced three spectacular winning teams.
In the 18+ Division, Miami JC Baseball Club made it a FOUR-PEAT with a win over the Boynton Black Sox. This is the fifth overall title for Miami JC.
In the 28+ Division, the Broward Senators are proving to be one the best teams ever in the SFBL, winning their fourth title in a row, with a win over the Miami Amigos. The Senators have now won TEN titles over the years.
In the 35+ Division, the Miami Yankees beat the Dade Nationals for the 35+ Division championship – Miami Yankees – 3-PEAT Champions and finished the season undefeated.
Each team won the $1,000 cash prize along with championship trophy, MVP trophy, champions t-shirts and authentic SFBL logo baseball caps for all the players, along with the eternal glory of winning an SFBL championship!

t: Boca Red Sox shutout Plantation Astros 8-0 to win 18+ Division Spring 2021 Championship!
b:
Boca Red Sox Shutout Plantation Astros 8-0, Win First SFBL Championship in Spring 2021.
The Boca Red Sox earned the reputation of being one of the best teams in all South Florida after beating the Plantation Astros 8-0 in 18+ Division Spring 2021 Season Championship held at Sunset Park December 19, 2021. Garrett Rogers earned MVP honors with his utter domination of the Astros, pitching a shutout and allowing only a handful of hits, while his teammates pounded out the required runs to ensure a smooth victory. Along the way, the Sox played fabulous defense with multiple double plays and error free baseball. On a side note, the Astros had been playing dominant baseball over the entire season posting a 9-2-1 record so the loss was a surprise. The shutout even more so.
The Boca Red Sox previously beat the Decar Senators 3-2 in 12 innings in the quarterfinals and the South Florida Rays 6-4 in the semifinals. In the other bracket, the Plantation Astros took out Team Hialeah 13-5 in the quarterfinals and then beat the defending champions FLOMO Crusaders 3-2 in the semifinals to advance to the finals.

t: Dade Nationals Defeat Southern Yankees in Masters Division Championship 12-6
b:
Dade Nationals – Masters Division Champions!
The Dade Nationals won the first ever Masters Division Championship with a resounding 12-6 win over the Southern Yankees at Sunset Park in Plantation June 23, 2019. The Nationals broke it open midway through the game and coasted to victory after MVP Alvin Rivera hit a two run BLAST to deep left field. Closer Alex Batista finished strong to snuff out a late Yankees rally before the Nationals broke out into their happy dance.
Veteran skipper, Jorge Orbeta Sr., has been after the crown for almost two decades but, finally, his claim to fame arrived! Can't say he didn't earn it.
Perennial contenders, Southern Yankees, were in the hunt all season, getting stronger and stronger each week, only to come so close in the Finals. But no one loves the game more than Yankees manager, Ray Asmar, who promises the Yankees will be back to take care of unfinished business.
Congratulations to the Dade Nationals, our MASTERS DIVISION CHAMPIONS! (Jorge Orbeta Sr. – Nationals Manager; Alvin Rivera – MVP; Ray Asmar – Southern Yankees Manager)

t: Miami Brewers OPEN DIVISION Champions – beat Fort Lauderdale Yankees in a thriller 8-6
b:
The Miami Brewers won their sixth Open Division Title, beating the previously undefeated 13-0 Fort Lauderdale Yankees 8-6 in a THRILLER. Down 4-1 in the eighth inning, the Brewers gave up two runs on wild pitches and looked down and out heading into the ninth inning losing 6-1. But after loading the bases on a base hit, walk and hit batter, the Yankees brought in their ace reliever with no outs. But the Brewers staged an epic comeback and scored 7 runs in the top of the ninth, all with two out, to take an 8-6 lead before closing it out in the bottom of the ninth 1-2-3.
What an awful way to end the season for the Yankees, who are clearly one of the best teams in league, but give the Brewers the credit for not giving up. The baseball gods were on their side this time and now the Brewers have claimed their sixth title. Sean Hernandez, the Brewers Ace, won the Most Valuable Player honors, pitching 7 innings, giving up only 6 hits with 9 strikeouts! Congratulations to both teams for their outstanding seasons!

t: Kendall Metz upset 7 time defending champs Palm Beach Angels 8-6 to Win Masters Championship
b: Down 6-0 in the top of the seventh, the Kendall Metz staged a late rally to upset the 7 time defending champions Palm Beach Angels, 8-6, to win the Fall 2017 Season Masters Championship. Metz Ace, Andrew Smiley, led the way, pitching 8 1/3 innings and hitting a two run triple and an RBI double to pull the Metz back to even with the Angels in the eighth inning before Alex Lopez hit a game-winning two run bomb to put the Metz up for good 8-6. The Metz, led by veteran PC Cabrera, were yelling "You Gotta Believe!" and "We Belong!". Angels Ace, Matt Leban, who has won numerous Angels championship games, was hurling another masterpiece through 6 1/3 innings, with one error spoiling a perfect game, only to fall victim to some uncharacteristic defensive lapses to open the floodgates for the Metz. Special mention to Jaime Siragusa and the Palm Beach Angels, who had won 7 titles and 80 games in a row prior to the upset in the most amazing championship run in SFBL history. These guys were invincible!

t: Angels FOUR-PEAT Masters Champions – beat Yankees 11-9 in a thriller!
b:
The Palm Beach Angels are FOUR-PEAT Masters Division Champions, surviving an intense nail biter 11-9 against the Miami Yankees in the 2015 Fall Season Masters Division Championship. Skipper Jamie Siragusa led the team to its fourth title in a row while Matt Leban earned MVP honors. The Angels CASHED IN, earning a Championship Trophy, MVP trophy, champions t-shirts and a $1000 CASH BONUS!
It was a heart breaker for the Yankees, who led 5-2 midway through the game on the strength of a bases loaded double followed by a three run BLAST! But errors opened the door for the Angels and they surged ahead 11-7, before the Yankees rallied in the ninth for two runs to lose 11-9. But Luis Cancino and his Miami Yankees club are now a force to be dealt with going forward. But it was an awesome championship at Sunset Park in Plantation, May 15, 2016.
Ibrahim Navarro AKA La Charla – Miami Yankees circled the bases after crushing a 3-run Home Run. Ryan Scott – Angels slugger also featured.

t: Brewers, Beach Bums, Sting Rays and Red Sox advance to the Division Championships!
b:
Two-time defending champions, the Miami Brewers, knocked off the surging Miami Orioles 16-9 and Miami Red Sox upset the top seed Miami Pirates 6-3 in the Dade Open Division Semifinals this past Sunday April 24. The Sox and the Brewers advance to the Dade Open Championship this coming Sunday May 1, 2016 at 9am at Flamingo Park Miami Beach.
In the Broward Open Division Semifinals, the South Florida Beach Bums surprised the top seeded Weston Sharks 12-3, while the South Florida Sting Rays punished the Davie Tigers by an identical score 12-3. The Beach Bums square off with the Sting Rays this coming Sunday May 1 at 930am at Sunset Park in Plantation. Let the Big Dog Eat!
Featured players: Collin Taylor (Miami Red Sox Ace); Chaz Lemoine (South Florida Sting Rays Ace); Michael Jordan aka MJ (star player, South Florida Beach Bums); Javier Rodriguez (Miami Brewers all star).

t: Palm Beach Angels Win 7th Masters Title in a Row
b:
The Palm Beach Angels beat the Miami Reds 7 – 3 in the Masters Division Finals to win their seventh championship in a row. The championship game was played at the beautiful Sunset Park Baseball Complex in Plantation Sunday, January 14, 2018.
The Angels dominated the game as Angels Ace, Matt Leban, kept the Reds in check all day, while the bats did the talking. Andres Gomez, star player for the Palm Beach Angels, won the MVP trophy. With the win, the Angels notched their 67th win in a row to extend the League's all-time winning streak. Abraham Lincoln was President the last time the Angels lost a ballgame. Hats off to skipper, Jamie Siragusa, MVP Andres Gomez and the Palm Beach Angels squad for setting the high bar.
The Angels earned a $1000 cash bonus, championship t-shirts and authentic SFBL baseball caps for the players…very nice!

The defending champions, Miami Brewers, came from behind to take out the Miami Dbacks in the first Senior Division semi-final game while the Davie Tigers edged out the South Florida Sting Rays in the second semi-final game. So that sets up a repeat of last season’s Senior Division Championship!  The Senior Division Championship is set for 930 am at McCarthur High in Hollywood.
The Palm Beach Angels, who won the title last season,  roughed up the Florida Yankees while the Miramar Rangers took out the Dade Nationals in the two Masters Division Semi-final games to set up another classic – Rangers vs Angels. The Masters Division Championship is set for 930 am at Sunset Park in Plantation.
The winners of each division will receive a $1,000 CASH PRIZE along with a championship trophy! Let the big dog eat!

Last season's Master's Division league MVP Rick Cabello has been traded from last season's champions Miramar Rangers to The Hollywood Twins for a player to be named later and future consideration. Rick pitched a brilliant complete game in the Masters Division championship game last season to beat The Twins 10-4.
Rick Cabello was quoted as saying "I loved playing for Felipe (Florentino) and The Rangers, but my new schedule was such that I couldn't travel to Dade County anymore. I wish The Rangers good luck."
Rick Cabello's Fall 2012 regular season stats:
Batting - Cabello, Rick: BA .486, PA 39, R 9, H 17, RBI 7, 2B 0, 3B 0, HR 0, SB 0, BB 4, K 3, HBP 0, Sac 0, AB 35, OBA .538, Slg .486.
Pitching - Cabello, Rick: W 4, L 4, S 0, ERA 3.38, Opp .275, IP 74 2/3, ER 28, H 85, BB 17, K 32, HB 4.

t: Orlando Bombers win Miami Open Baseball Tournament, beat Rays 8-2 in Final
b:
The Orlando Bombers won the 2016 Miami Open Baseball Tournament , beating the South Florida Sting Rays 8-2 in the Championship. After losing to the Sting Rays in the opening game, Skipper Gabe Gonzalez’s squad pulled off an impressive win over the Orlando Madness 5-3 and then routed the Miami Red Sox to climb into the final. And after falling behind early to the Sting Rays in the final, the Bombers put together a string of hits to take the lead before winning the game going away.
Shown below Orlando Bombers in their new Championship T-Shirts!
The Miami Open , presented by South Florida Baseball League, is open to any and all teams regardless of affiliation and takes place in Miami, Florida on Memorial Day weekend each year. Contact the SFBL at playball@sfbl.com for more details.

t: Dade Nationals earn 35+ Division Spring 2021 Season Title with 17-6 win over South Florida Dodgers
b:
The Dade Nationals took out the South Florida Dodgers 17-6 to win the 35+ Division Spring 2021 Season Championship at Miami Christian High December 5, 2021. The Nationals claimed their third consecutive and fourth overall titles to lay down their legacy of supremacy. Star shortstop and pitcher, Roylan Lozano - Dade Nationals, was named Most Valuable Player for playing like a grown man among boys. But the story is really about a bunch of players that have played together for years and years and lately that experience and consistency, well that's just hard to beat.
Meanwhile, the South Florida Dodgers, who squeezed by the Boca Mets 15-14 in a wild semifinals game, have now been runner-up in two finals but expect to be back in the finals again next season to finish the job!

t: South Florida Cubs beat Weston Leones 3-1, Claim Open Division Title
b:
Backed by the strong pitching performance turned in by Garret Rogers, the South Florida Cubs held off the Weston Leones to win the OPEN DIVISION title in a tough 3-1 contest. The championship game, which was played at Sunset Park in Plantation Sunday June 9, featured gripping baseball and tense moments, with two highly competitive teams going at it for all marbles. But newcomers to the league, South Florida Cubs shut the door when it counted and claimed the crown.
Garret Rogers won the MVP trophy and the South Florida Cubs won a beautiful team trophy and championship SFBL t-shirts and caps as well as the $1000 cash prize! Nicely done Cubs!
South Florida Cubs – SFBL FALL 2018 SEASON OPEN DIVISION CHAMPIONS
Garret Rogers FALL 2018 SEASON MVP
South Florida Cubs CHAMPS!

t: Davie Tigers and Palm Beach Angels new Champs!
b:
The Davie Tigers won the Senior Division Crown and the Palm Beach Angels claimed the Masters Division Title for the 2014 Spring Season in convincing style. Both teams swept their best-of-three championship series 2 games to zero, with the Angels taking out the Miramar Rangers and the Tigers outlasting the South Florida Beach Bums.
Congratulations to our new champions - Palm Beach Angels and the Davie Tigers! Hats off the gritty South Florida Beach Bums, who ended up in the championship series after a very slow start, and the defending champions, Miramar Rangers, a team which seems to live in the championship series every season.
Spring 2014 Season Champions:
Premier Division - Coral Gables Athletics
Senior Division - Davie Tigers
Masters Division - Palm Beach Angels

t: Miami Brewers Win Another Open 18 PLUS Division Title 10-6 over Delray Nationals
b:
Brewers Skipper, George Kenhan (left), and Sean Hernandez, MVP, enjoy another big time win!
After a brief hiatus, the Miami Brewers proved their durability by returning to the OPEN 18 PLUS Division Finals Sunday, December 8, 2019, at Plantation Sunset Park to match up with a tough Delray Nationals team. And, yes, the Brewskies won another title, capitalizing on uncharacteristic sloppy defense, and blasting the Nationals 10-6. Brewers ace, Sean Hernandez, won the MVP honors, pitching a complete game and leading the Brew Crew all season. Delray Nationals catcher and team manager, Ryan Snyder, hit a long bomb in the losing effort to highlight the Nats offense.
Ladies and Gentlemen …. the BREW CREW – Spring 2019 OPEN 18 PLUS DIVISION CHAMPIONS!

t: Davie Tigers beat Miami Brewers 7 - 2 to claim their third title
b:
The Davie Tigers beat the Miami Brewers 7 - 2 in the Open Division Finals to win their third championship. The championship game was played at the beautiful Flamingo Park Baseball Stadium Miami Beach Sunday January 7, 2018.
The Tigers jumped out to a four run lead to break open a 0-0 game in the fourth inning, only to watch the Brewers come back with two runs in the sixth and then left the bases loaded to pull to a 4-2 score. The Tigers put the game away with three more runs to close out with a 7-2 final. Tigers manager, Dave Carnevale, collected more hardware, while newcomer, Jose won the MVP trophy.
The Tigers earned a $1000 cash bonus, championship t-shirts and authentic SFBL baseball caps for the players...nice.

t: OPEN DIVISION PLAYOFFS – Tigers Get By Beach Bums and Sharks survive Pirates in Semi Finals
b:
The Davie Tigers beat back a furious South Florida Beach Bums rally pulling away in the top of the ninth with an 11 – 7 win in the first game of the OPEN DIVISION SEMI FINALS this past Sunday Dec 4. In the second Semi Final game, the Weston Sharks needed every bit of everything to get by the Miami Pirates 6 – 5 in the ninth.
This sets the stage for the OPEN DIVISION CHAMPIONSHIP this coming Sunday December 11 – DAVIE TIGERS VS WESTON SHARKS. Don't miss it!
Shown above, Davie Tigers skipper, Dave Carnevale, slides into third base on his way to a BIG PLAYOFF WIN! Shown below – Davie Tigers getting ready to celebrate on the mound after securing a hard fought win over the Beach Bums in the Open Division Semi Finals.

t: Miami Brewers beat Miami Red Sox 5-1 now going for THREE-PEAT!
b:
The surging Miami Red Sox were shut down by Patrick Chamberlain and the Miami Brewers 5-1 at Flamingo Park Miami Beach in the Dade Open Conference Championship this past Sunday May, 2016. Patrick threw a three hitter and struck out 14 batters in a complete game effort. A studly performance! The Brewers are now on a quest to THREE-PEAT, having won the championship the past two seasons! They take on a powerful South Florida Sting Rays team that has been itching for a title for the past three seasons in the Open Division Championship to be held May 22, 2016.
Shown below – George Kenhan, player-manager of the Miami Brewers; Bryan Probst Miami Brewers monster shortstop; Patrick Chamberlain Miami Brewers Ace.

t: Angels win 6th Consecutive Masters Division Title
b: The Palm Beach Angels proved, once again, that they are simply unbeatable having just taken down the Miramar Rangers 13-2 in the Fall 2016 Season Masters Division Championship. This makes it six, count ’em, six in a row for the Halos. Tony Krahwinkle hammered a three run bomb to deep centerfield in the first inning to stake the Angels a 4-0 lead and then Alvaro Gomez smashed a three-run walk-off home run to deep right field in the eighth inning to end the contest. Tony Krahwinkle was voted Most Valuable Player and Jamie Siragusa, Angels manager, needed to rent a new warehouse just to store all the trophies he’s been collecting. Congratulations Angels!

In the 18+ Division, the Flomo Crusaders won the fall 2021 championship by defeating the Boca Red Sox 7-5, earning their first SFBL trophy. In the 28+Division, the championship game between the Miami Amigos and Miami Yankees was vacated due to rules irregularities. The Dade Nationals went 9-2 in the 35+Division in the regular season, and then clinched their fifth title in a row. They beat the Miami Cardinals 8-2.

t: Hollywood Astros Win 28+ Division Spring 2021 Season Crown beat Miami Mariners 13-6
b:
The Hollywood Astros completed their Cinderella Season by thumping the Miami Mariners 13-6 in the 28+ Division Spring 2021 Season finals held at Sunset Park December 12, 2021. Powered by two early inning home runs, including a GRAND SLAM, the Astros took a commanding lead and survived a late challenge to win decisively. Anthony Aragon, ace pitcher for the Astros, took the MVP honors.
Entering the post season as the fourth seed, the Astros beat the top seed Miami Amigos 9-6 in the semifinals to set the tone. The 2 Seed Mariners had outlasted the Miami Yankees 7-6 in extra innings in the other semifinals game to get to the finals.

t: Sting Rays hold off fierce Brewers rally in the ninth win Open Division Championship 7-6
b:
The South Florida Sting Rays beat the two-time defending champions, Miami Brewers, to claim their first Open Division Championship 7-6 at Sunset Park in Plantation May 22, 2016. Chaz Lemoine, the wiley Sting Rays veteran, earned the League’s MVP trophy. The Sting Rays earned a $1000 CASH BONUS as well as championship trophy and tshirts! Congratulations Sting Rays on a job well done! Stay tuned – story to follow!
Below: Eric Abend – Sting Rays breaks into his home run trot after crushing a decisive 3-run blast to lead the Rays over the Brewers!
Below: Two time defending champions – Miami Brewers almost had the three-peat!

t: FLOMO Crusaders WIN OPEN CHAMPIONSHIP over Beach Bums 9-5
b:
The FLOMO Crusaders defeated the South Florida Beach Bums 9-5 to WIN the OPEN DIVISION CHAMPIONSHIP! The Crusaders were led by MVP Ace, Rody Mederos, who won his third playoff game in a row to lead the Crusaders to their first title. The Bums were stymied by a steady rain before the game was called early in the eighth inning due to the field getting flooded. That washed out any Bums hopes for a late inning rally.
Congratulations to Coaches Juan Carlos, William, Ricardo Gonzalez and Tino Burges for a job well done and Open Title secured! FLOMO Crusaders are our Fall 2017 Open Division Champions!

t: Davie Tigers Beat Weston Sharks 3-0 to win the Open Division Championship
b:
The Davie Tigers put together a masterful game, complete with excellent pitching and defense and timely hitting to beat top-seeded Weston Sharks 3-0 on their way to an Open Division Championship Sunday December 18, 2016 at the beautiful Flamingo Park Miami Beach.
This is the Tigers second title and skipper Dave Carnevale couldn't be happier as he took home the Championship Trophy, the $1000 Cash Prize for the team, while his teammates each received championship t-shirts and authentic custom embroidered SFBL baseball caps. Rene Arauz, Davie Tigers ace shown below, took home the MVP Award.

Four time defending champions, Palm Beach Angels, are making it a habit collecting all the hardware and software available at the South Florida Baseball League. Once again, the mighty Angels are in the Masters Division Finals, only this time they have to take on a new comer to the finals, the feisty South Florida Dodgers!
Don’t miss the game this coming Sunday January 8, 2017 at 9am at Sunset Park in Plantation. At stake are the Championship and MVP trophies, championship t-shirts and authentic SFBL logo caps along with a $1000 Cash Prize!

t: OPEN DIVISION CHAMPIONSHIP – Brewers vs Sharks – Sunday June 25
b:
The Open Division Championship is this coming Sunday June 25, 2017 at 9 am at the beautiful Flamingo Park Stadium in Miami Beach. Come out and watch the former champs, Miami Brewers, take on the top seed Weston Sharks in a battle royale! The Brewers beat the White Sox 6-5 and the Sharks beat the Bums 7-4 to reach the finals.
The winning team earns a $1000 CASH PRIZE, a championship trophy, an MVP trophy and championship tshirts and authentic SFBL logo baseball caps for the players! LET THE BIG DOG EAT!

t: Miami Brewers beat Miami Pirates 9-3 to advance to Open Division Finals
b:
The Miami Brewers beat the Miami Pirates 9-3 at beautiful Flamingo Park Miami Beach Sunday December 17 to advance to the Open Division Finals. The Brewers are no strangers to the Finals having been there four times out of the past five season. Awaiting them in the Finals are the Davie Tigers, who previously came from behind to knock out the South Florida Beach Bums 3-2.
Pictured below is Brandon Sam – Miami Brewers OF P

t: Kendall Metz take on Palm Beach Angels in Masters Championship
b: The SFBL presents the Masters Division Championship between the Kendall Metz and the defending champions, Palm Beach Angels this Sunday, July 22. The game will take place at Sabal Pines Park in Plantation with first pitch at 10 am. The Angels look to make it 8 championships in a row and the Metz hope to ride a horse named Smiley to the promised land. Let's rumble! Shown below - Kendall Metz Ace, Andrew Smiley.

t: Miami Rays challenge Coral Gables Athletics in Premier Championship
b: The Miami Rays are looking to upset two-time defending champions Coral Gables Athletics this Sunday October 12 at Flamingo Park Miami Beach in the final game of the 3-game Premier Division Championship Series. The two teams split the opening day doubleheader with the Rays winning 3-2 in the opener and the A's coming back and winning 4-2 in the second game to even the series 1-1. Let the big dog eat!

t: Davie Tigers beat Delray Nationals 13-10 in OPENING GAME of Fall 2017 Season
b:
While they wait to play the Finals January 7, the Davie Tigers took care of a little business by winning the OPENING GAME of the new Fall 2017 Season over the Delray Nationals by a score of 13-10. New Season for the entire league opens January 7, 2018. ROCK N ROLL!
Shown below is Delray Nationals Ace Carlos Fleury.

SFBL League Stat Sheet (Spring 2003 template) — official statistics reporting form.
Header: South Florida Baseball League, 5681 SW 59th Place, South Miami, Florida 33143, www.sfbl.com. "STATISTICS SHEET FOR ___ (Team Name)", Game # ___ / ___ / ___.
Reporting instructions:
1. Complete all sections. Please print legibly!
2. Fax/email form by 6:00 PM on Monday following each game.
3. Fax to the league office at 305 666-5722 or email to: FSKLAND@BELLSOUTH.NET
4. Scouting reports / team stats available upon request.
Dade fields: BEACH, GABLES, KILL, COPRK, MDCC, SOUTH, WPERR, HOME CHRIS. Broward fields: BOCA, TAM, SBH, STTHOM, SUNSET, STRANAHAN. Game time options: AM / NOON / PM / NIGHT. Umpires 1/2/3, Home Team, Visiting Team.
Batting stat columns: LEAG, COUNTY, TEAM, PLAYER'S NAME, #, GP, AB, R, H, RBI, 2B, 3B, HR, SB, BB, K, HBP, SAC.
Line score: Winning Team / Losing Team — RUNS, HITS, ERRORS.
Pitching columns (Your Pitchers 1-4): IP, ER, H, BB, K, HB, W, L.
Also: Comments to the league office; Names of ejected players (1, 2).
(Same form as the Fall 2002 sheet but with the updated South Miami league-office address and phone. Workbook authored by Dean Cosme, last saved by Miguel, Microsoft Excel; last saved Jan 30 2003.)

t: New playoff roster guidelines / rosters now public
b:
All team rosters can now be viewed. Please visit the Stats / Login menu to view team rosters.
Playoff rosters are finalized after game 10 is completed. Players added after game 10 will NOT be eligible for playoff games. Players must play a minimum of 3 games to be eligible for playoff competition.

The SFBL Leaderboards have moved to Net Retrieval! Please visit the new Leaderboards Page.

This page honors those players who turn in outstanding performances during the course of the SFBL season. Instructions on how to nominate a player are listed below. Team managers and SFBL players are encouraged to nominate deserving players each Monday of the season. Please send photos and videos of your nominee.
Every once in a while someone turns in an extraordinary performance. So the SFBL created the “BASEBALL BEAST AWARD” for the best individual performance of the season as voted by the league members the end of the regular season. The SFBL conducts the special poll on Facebook to determine the winner, who will earn the BEAST trophy, an authentic SFBL logo baseball cap and permanent spot in the history books.
Instructions on how to nominate a player of the week:
1. Email the league office at playball@sfbl.com.
2. Please include the player’s full name, team name and his stats for the week along with photo and, if possible, action shots.

This page honors those players who turn in outstanding performances during the course of the SFBL season.  Instructions on how to nominate a player are listed below.  Team managers and SFBL players are encouraged to nominate deserving players each Monday of the season.  Please send photos and videos of your nominee.
Every once in a while someone turns in an extraordinary performance. Going forward, the SFBL will present the “BASEBALL BEAST AWARD” for the best individual performance of the season as voted by the league members the end of the regular season. The SFBL will conduct the special poll on Facebook to determine the winner, who will earn the BEAST trophy, an authentic SFBL logo baseball cap and permanent spot in the history books.
Instructions on how to nominate a player of the week:
1. Email the league office at playball@sfbl.com.
2. Please include the player’s full name, team name and his stats for the week along with photo and, if possible, action shots.

This page honors those players who turn in outstanding performances during the course of the SFBL season.  Instructions on how to nominate a player are listed below.  Team managers and SFBL players are encouraged to nominate deserving players each Monday of the season.  Please send photos and videos of your nominee.
Every once in a while someone turns in an extraordinary performance. Going forward, the SFBL will present the “BASEBALL BEAST AWARD” for the best individual performance of the season as voted by the league members the end of the regular season. The SFBL will conduct the special poll on Facebook to determine the winner, who will earn the BEAST trophy, an authentic SFBL logo baseball cap and permanent spot in the history books.
Instructions on how to nominate a player of the week:
1. Email the league office at playball@sfbl.com.
2. Please include the player’s full name, team name and his stats for the week along with photo and, if possible, action shots.

Player of the Week for Fall 2019 Season AND NEW SEASON being finalized – check back soon. South Florida Baseball League presents Players of the Week for Fall 2019 Season AND the new Fall 2020 Season – in process.
Player of the Week 12 Spring 2019 – Rodney Riera – FLOMO Crusaders, who threw a complete game in the championship game against the mighty Miami Amigos, winning 6-1 while striking out (batters).
Player of the Week 11 – Tony "Doc" Blanco – Miami Cardinals, who pitched a BIG TIME GEM to lead his team to a MASTERS DIVISION SEMI-FINALS WIN.
Players of the Week 10 – Bryan Ocana and Andy Perez – Miami Amigos, who threw a combined no-hitter against the feisty Kendall Metz.
Player of the Week 9 – Gus Castillo – Miami Amigos, who was just coming back from labrum surgery and, like the Bionic Man, went 4-4 with 3 doubles.
Player of the Week 7 – Eduardo Llovero – Delray Nationals, who turned in a Superman Performance against the Weston Leones in early season action. Eduardo went 4-5.
Player of the Week 6 – Everett Miller – South Florida Beach Bums, who made an emergency start despite a massive ankle bruise. He pitched a complete game with 15 (strikeouts).
Player of the Week 5 – Eric Harper – Coral Springs Royals, who went 6 for 6, with two monster doubles and 4 runs batted in to lead his Coral Springs Royals.

t: Player of the Week 2 – Arnaldo Rincones – Miami Diamondbacks
b:
Hit Zone Batting Cages presents Player of the Week 2:
Arnaldo Rincones – Miami Diamondbacks, went 2-for-3, with TWO HOME RUNS and 6 RBI, in an 18-6 loss to the Miami Red Sox! What a Monster. You could hang laundry on the frozen ropes he crushed off of Red Sox Ace, Collin Taylor. After driving in a run on a laser beam line out to right, Arnaldo got serious, crushing a two run homer in the third and a three run bomb in the fifth before the mercy rule kept him from hitting another.
Hitting lasers is what he does – Arnaoldo Rincones, our Player of the Week 2!  Way to put on the MONSTER MASH!
Setting the tone for the season, Arnaldo Rincones - our Player of the Week 2!
Arnaldo won a free hour at the batting cages courtesy of Hit Zone – Miami Tropical Park.
Hit Zone
Address:  7900 Bird Rd, Miami, FL 33155
Phone:  (305) 448-9663
Hours:  Daily 2:00 – 9:00 pm
Tags: featuredonfront

t: Player of the Week 6 – John Emmons
b:
Hit Zone Batting Cages presents Player of the Week 6:
John Emmons – Miami Diamondbacks, pitched seven innings, giving up no earned runs against the Miami Red Sox to pick up the win, then came back in the ninth to pick up the “unofficial save”! What a a classic application of the pitcher re-entry rule! Then again, John Emmons does not need any help. The man has won more baseball games than just about anybody in the East Coast of the USA. That’s no lie. John Emmons is also one of the Ancients!!
Always dominating, and always especially pleased with great defense, John Emmons, our Player of the Week 6!
Winning!!  John Emmons – our Player of the Week 6!
John won a free hour at the batting cages courtesy of Hit Zone – Miami Tropical Park.
Hit Zone
Address:  7900 Bird Rd, Miami, FL 33155
Phone:  (305) 448-9663
Hours:  Daily 2:00 – 9:00 pm

t: Player of the Week 9 – Marcos Calderon
b:
Hit Zone Batting Cages presents Player of the Week 9:
Marcos Calderon – Miami Red Sox, turned in one helluva performance, going 3-4 with a Grand Slam and a double, and collecting 5 RBI while pitching one inning in clutch relief the game on the line.  Down 7-4 in the eighth, Marcos smashed a GRAND SLAM to lead the Miami Red Sox to a 9-8 come-from-behind win in 11 innings over the previously undefeated South Florida Sting Rays. Marcos Calderon taking charge!
Beating up on the league already, New Kid in Town – Marcos Calderon, our Player of the Week 9!
Leading the comeback, Marcos Calderon – our Player of the Week 9!
Marcos won a free hour at the batting cages courtesy of Hit Zone – Miami Tropical Park.
Hit Zone
Address:  7900 Bird Rd, Miami, FL 33155
Phone:  (305) 448-9663
Hours:  Daily 2:00 – 9:00 pm

Bases Loaded Batting Cages presents Player of the Week 11:
Juan Vene Machado – Deerfield White Sox - had one extraordinary day at the office.  Actually, an unbelievable-you-had-to-be-there-to-believe-it day at the office. And what are we talking about? How about a four for five performance with three, count ‘em, three triples and a double and five RBI in the White Sox win over the Southern Yankees in the Senior Division. Hitting one triple in a game is an awesome experience. Well Juan went where very few men have ever gone. What a game to remember and what a pounding he put on the other team. Way to go Juan – a studly performance if there ever was!
Juan has also won a free hour of batting cage time at Bases Loaded Batting Cages. Visit www.GoBasesLoaded.com.
Tags: featuredonfront

t: Player of the Week 1 – Ahmad Williams – Fort Lauderdale Yankees
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents our Spring Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 1 – Ahmad Williams – Fort Lauderdale Yankees , who turned in one of the best performances in the league in a long time. First, he pitched 5 1/3 innings in relief after entering the game in the third inning down 6-0, recording 9 K, giving up ZERO earned runs and only 2 hits. He also mashed at the plate, going 3-4 with 2 doubles, 1 triple, 1 BB and 4 RBI.
Talk about drama, Ahmad also had the go ahead bases-loaded double to lead the Yankees to an 11-7 win over the Miami RH Baseball Club.
AND, if that’s not enough, he also threw the “IMMACULATE INNING, striking out three batters on 9 pitches.
Doing it all, throwing BB’s and hitting frozen ropes, Ahmad Williams, our Player of the Week 1!

t: Player of the Week 5 – Chaz Lemoine
b:
Hit Zone Batting Cages presents Player of the Week 5:
Chaz Lemoine – South Florida Sting Rays, put on a clinic against the Miami Diamondbacks, going 4-4 at the plate and pitching a complete game shutout, while striking out 10.  Here’s the secret no one seems to remember…this guy has been winning player of the week for the past 24 years!  Chaz Lemoine is one of the Ancients!!
Still going strong, Chaz Lemoine, our Player of the Week 5!  Congratulations on another one, Chaz!
Weaving another masterpiece, Chaz Lemoine – our Player of the Week 5!

t: Player of the Week 4 – Bryan Probst – Miami Brewers
b:
South Florida Baseball League  presents our Spring Season   PLAYER OF THE WEEK 4 – Bryan Probst – Miami Brewers ,  who keeps showing up on the Player of the Week Table every season! HOW does he do it? Speak softly and carry a BIG STICK!   Bryan showed up for work, going 3-3, with a double and a triple and raked in 6 RBI. Good grief.
The one-man wrecking crew, Bryan Probst,   our   Player of the Week 4!

t: Player of the Week 9 – Gus Castillo – Miami Amigos
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents Players of the Week 9 – Gus Castillo – Miami Amigos, who was just coming back from labrum surgery and, like the Bionic Man, went 4-4, with 3 doubles, 3 stolen bases and 2 diving plays at second base in an amazing 5 tools performance!
We don’t get many 5 TOOL PERFORMANCES so they are RARE! Three words explain our sentiment – Well done Gus! – Player of the Week 9!

t: Player of the Week 5 – Eric Harper – Coral Springs Royals
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents Player of the Week 5 – Eric Harper – Coral Springs Royals, who went 6 for 6, with two monster doubles and 4 runs batted in to lead his Coral Spring Royals to an impressive 12-6 win over the Boca Mets! What a day at the office. Eric was just showing off his lumber. Nice Wood.
Beating up on other teams is what he does best, Eric Harper, our Player of the Week 5.

t: Player of the Week 2 – Ryan Quintero – South Florida Beach Bums
b:
The South Florida Baseball League presents our Spring Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 2 – Ryan Quintero – South Florida Beach Bums, who went 2-5, with a double and 3 RBI at the plate while pitching 4 innings in relief with 8 strikeouts to power the Bum’s thrilling 6-run comeback to tie the top ranked Miami Pirates 7-7.
Keeping them in the game and saving the day, Ryan Quintero, our Player of the Week 2!

t: Player of the Week 2 – David Hopwood, South Florida Dodgers
b:
The South Florida Baseball League presents our Fall Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 2 – David Hopwood, player-manager of the South Florida Dodgers, who went 2-2, with 3 walks, 4 RBI and THE GRAND SALAMI! Every player in the league dreams of slugging a bases-loaded bomb – well young David got one!
Congratulations to David Hopwood, our deserving Player of the Week 2 for showing the rest of us the glory!

Congratulations go out to a pair of Angels, Phil Laufman and  Fernando Urtiaga, who led their Palm Beach Angels to a 20-7 come-from-behind win over Miramar Rangers.  Phil went 4-4 with 5 RBI to earn Week 3 Player of the Week while fellow teammate Fernando went 4-4 with 2 RBI to earn Honorable Mention.
Phil and Fernando also have won 1 free hour of batting cage time at a location of their choice.

t: Player of the Week 10 – Cory Hopper, Miami Sox
b:
The South Florida Baseball League presents our Fall Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 10 – Cory Hopper – Miami Red Sox, who struck out 22 batters in a thrilling 9-8 Red Sox win over the defending champions, Davie Tigers. WOW, what a performance! Let that soak in – 22K – a feat that simply will not repeated for a long, long time.
Turning in one UNREAL PERFORMANCE, Cory Hopper, our Player of the Week 10!

t: Player of the Week 6 – Cesar Carillo, Davie Tigers
b:
The South Florida Baseball League presents our Fall Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 6 – Cesar Carillo, Davie Tigers, who pitched a complete game and picked up an 11-1 win over the Hialeah Astros, while also going 4-5 with a double at the plate. Cesar put on a clinic on how to be a one man show and the Astros do NOT want to see him again.
What a day it was for Ceasar Carillo, our Player of the Week 6!

t: Player of the Week 6 – Andrew Smiley – Kendall Metz
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents our Fall Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 6 – Andrew Smiley – Kendall Metz, who pitched a 9 inning complete game with 15 strikeouts while giving up zero earned runs in the Metz 6-3 win over the Miami Reds. This makes the second time in two seasons that Mr. Smiley earned Player of the Week. Not bad.
Dealing and slinging it, Andrew Smiley, our Player of the Week 6!

Bases Loaded Batting Cages presents Player of the Week 7: Daniel McNutt of the Boca Marlins became a one-man wrecking machine. Dan tore it up against the Florida Yankees, going 5-6 with a triple, two doubles and 5 RBI, and pitched 5 strong innings. Looking like an All Star, Dan the Man is our Player of the Week 7 and has won a free hour of batting cage time at Bases Loaded Batting Cages.

t: Player of the Week 8 – Carlos Pandolfo – South Florida Dodgers
b:
The South Florida Baseball League presents our Spring Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 8 – Carlos Pandolfo – South Florida Dodgers, who drove in the tying run in the top of the tenth to set up a walk off win while pitching a 10 inning complete game to get the WIN against the Kendall Metz.
Sprinkle a little of that walk off magic on the rest of us, Carlos Pandolfo, our Player of the Week 8!

t: Player of the Week 4 – Mike Solerno, Miami Red Sox
b:
The South Florida Baseball League presents our Fall Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 4 – Mike Solerno, shortstop for the Miami Red Sox, who went 4-5, with 3 RBI, 3 Runs and  a double to set the tone in a big Red Sox win. Working all night and then stroking the wood by day.  What a gamer!
Hitting frozen ropes by force of habit, congratulations to Mike Solerno, our Player of the Week 4!

t: Fall 2018 Player of the Week 5 – Bruce Michelson – Boca Mets
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents Fall 2018 Season Player of the Week 5 – Bruce Michelson – Boca Mets, who, at age 64, got a ninth inning clutch base hit and scored the game winning run on a passed ball that culminated in a wild play at the plate covered in a cloud of dust. GAME WINNER!
Showing no fear, our own ageless wonder –Bruce Michelson, our Player of the Week 5!

t: Player of the Week 9 – Alex Aguirre – Ft Lauderdale Yankees
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents our Fall Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 9 – Alex Aguirre – Fort Lauderdale Yankees, who pitched a one hitter – complete 9 inning game with 5 K and only one walk in a thrilling 1-0 win over the Miami Pirates.  Seven times he put the Pirates down 1-2-3!
Doing his best PICASSO and pitching a MASTERPIECE, Alex Aguirre, our Player of the Week 9!

t: Player of the Week 4 – Adrian Labady – Miami Yankees
b:
The South Florida Baseball League presents our Spring Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 4 – Adrian Labady – Miami Yankees, who hit a WALK-OFF SINGLE in the bottom of the ninth to give the new expansion team Miami Yankees an opening day come-from-behind 13-12 WIN over a perennial playoff team South Florida Beach Bums.
Hitting WALK-OFF MAGIC, Adrian Labady, our Player of the Week 4!

t: Player of the Week 3 – John “Big Papi” Delgado, Miami Red Sox
b:
The South Florida Baseball League presents our Fall Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 3 – John “Big Papi” Delgado, first baseman Miami Red Sox, who went 3-5, with 6 RBI, 3 Runs and  a double to lead his team to victory!  Nice day at the office, Mr. Delgado – way to use the big stick.
Driving in runs is what he does best, congratulations to John Delgado our Player of the Week 3!

t: Player of the Week 8 – Big Papi Delgado – Miami Cardinals
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents our Spring Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 8 – John Big Papi Delgado – Miami Cardinals, who became a repeat player of the week with his awesome 4-4, 2B performance to power the Miami Cardinals.  Do not mess with the big man, he will hurt you.
Putting his bat in play is what he does best, John Big Papi Delgado, our Player of the Week 8!

t: Fall 2018 Player of the Week 9 – Rody Mederos – FLOMO Crusaders
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents Fall 2018 Season Player of the Week 9 – Rody Mederos – FLOMO Crusaders, who put on a magic show to throw a NO HITTER against the Miami Red Sox! This guy mesmerized everyone but he took no prisoners.
Congratulations to our former Baseball Beast Award recipient for earning our Player of the Week 9 honors – Rody Mederos!

t: Player of the Week 7 – Eduardo Llovero – Delray Nationals
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents Players of the Week 7 – Eduardo Llovero – Delray Nationals, who turned in a Superman Performance against the Weston Leones in a early season action. Eduardo went 4-5 with two doubles and a triple with 4 RBI. Not a bad day at all.
Pounding and mashing baseballs for a hobby, Eduardo Llovero, our Player of the Week 7!

t: Player of the Week 12 – Lander Suarez – Weston Leones
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents our Spring Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 12 – Lander Suarez- Weston Leones, who hit a clutch WALK OFF solo Home Run in the bottom of the ninth to lead the Weston Leones to a 7-6 win over the Broward Mariners in the OPEN Division Semifinals game.
Never forget that clutch homer, Lander Suarez, our Player of the Week 12!

t: Player of the Week 4 – Phillip Castillo – Broward Mariners
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents Players of the Week 4 – Phillip Castillo – Broward Mariners, who hit two monster home runs against the South Florida Cubs. And now he is known for his bear hunting skills – a real life Cubs killer!
Phillip Castillo – one of the League’s best power hitters and our Player of the Week 4!
Congratulations big dog!

t: Player of the Week 11 – Eric Sutcliffe – South Florida Bums
b:
The South Florida Baseball League presents our Spring Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 11 – Eric Sutcliffe – South Florida Bums, whose go ahead 2 run single in the top of the ninth broke open a 5-5 tie propelling the Beach Bums into the final spot for Open Division playoffs.
Count on him when the chips are down, Eric Sutcliffe, our Player of the Week 11!

t: Fall 2018 Player of the Week 1 – Adrian Roznowski – South Florida Rays
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents Fall 2018 Season Player of the Week 1 – Adrian Roznowski – South Florida Sting Rays, who had a PERFECT OPENING DAY GAME, going 4-4 with two monster doubles and 2 RBI. Setting the tone for the South Florida Rays, Adrian Roznowski showing us all how its done!
Congratulations on being PLAYER OF THE WEEK!

t: Fall 2018 Player of the Week 10 – Mike Clark – Broward Mariners
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents Fall 2018 Season Player of the Week 10 – Mike Clark – Broward Mariners, who went 2-2 with a 2 run home run and a GRAND SLAM, 2 walks and 6 RBI. What a nice bit of output. A one man scoring machine.
Hitting it out is what he does. Congratulations are in order for our Player of the Week 10 – Mike Clark!

t: Player of the Week 10 – Daniel Jordan – Miami Hurricanes
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents our Spring Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 10 – Daniel Jordan – Miami Hurricanes, who had one helluva day mashing the baseball, collecting 4 RBI while smashing a two-run home run.
Hogging the spotlight and making sure he powers his Hurricanes into the playoffs, Daniel Jordan, our Player of the Week 10!

t: Player of the Week 5 – Oliver Holsing – Miami Red Sox
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents our Spring Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 5 – Oliver Holsing – Miami Red Sox, who went 5-for-8 with two doubles, a triple, 3 RBI and 5 stolen bases in a Red Sox doubleheader. Not a bad day at the office.
Having a field day, all day. Give him a big “AttaBoy!,” Oliver Holsing, our Player of the Week 5!

t: Fall 2018 Player of the Week 11 – Joe Iacobucci – Boca Red Sox
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents Fall 2018 Season Player of the Week 11 – Joe Iacobucci – Boca Red Sox, who put in a perfect day at the plate, going 4-4 with two doubles and a Home Run, while collecting 3 RBI on the day. This is routine for Joe – just doing his job.
Congratulations to our Player of the Week 11 – Joe Iacobucci!

t: Player of the Week 10 – Andrew Smiley – Kendall Metz
b:
The South Florida Baseball League presents our Spring Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 10 – Andrew Smiley – Kendall Metz, who pitched a complete game with 20 strikeouts to continue his domination on the mound. A man among boys on a weekly basis.
Living rent free in the minds of the league hitters, Andrew Smiley, our Player of the Week 10!

t: Player of the Week 8 – Evan Lopez – Miami Red Sox
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents our Fall Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 8 – Evan Lopez – Miami Red Sox, who doubled and hit a GRAND SLAM, driving in 4 runs in a Red Sox loss to the South Florida Rays.  He also pitched 3 innings in relief.
Playing the game the old fashion way – power hitting, Evan Lopez, our Player of the Week 8!

t: Player of the Week 4 – Codi Manning – Delray Nationals
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents our Fall Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 4 – Codi Manning – Delray Nationals, who went 4-4 with a GRAND SLAM and also pitched 4 scoreless innings is a shortened game in the Nationals 15-1 win over the Miami BayStars.
Hitting and pitching like an all-star, Codi Manning, our Player of the Week 4!

t: Player of the Week 5 – Alvin Rivera – Dade Nationals
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents our Fall Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 5 – Alvin Rivera – Dade Nationals, who doubled and homered, driving in 4 runs.  He also struck out 4 in 2 innings pitched in the Nationals 9-8 win over the Miami Mariners.
Throwing down the hammer when it counted, Alvin Rivera, our Player of the Week 5!

t: Player of the Week 1 – Neil Sullivan – Miami Reds
b:
The South Florida Baseball League presents our Spring Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 1 – Neil Sullivan – Miami Reds, who pitched a Complete Game to pick up the OPENING DAY 7-4 WIN over the Dade Red Sox, while collecting two doubles, a single and 3 RBI.
Way to lead the opening day charge Neil Sullivan, our Player of the Week 1!

t: Player of the Week 3 – Mike Hughes – South Florida Beach Bums
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents our Fall Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 3 – Mike Hughes – South Florida Beach Bums, who pitched a one hitter, struck out 9 and gave up no runs over 5 innings in the Beach Bums to a 14-2 win over the South Florida Marlins.
Throwing BB’s all day long, Mike Hughes, our Player of the Week 3!

t: Player of the Week 3 – Adrian Roznowski – South Florida Rays
b:
The South Florida Baseball League presents our Spring Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 3 – Adrian Roznowski – South Florida Rays, who went 3-4, with a home run and a double and 3 RBI to power his team over the Hialeah Astros 11-7. What a day at the office!
The hiting machine, Adrian Roznowski, our Player of the Week 3!

Hit Zone Batting Cages presents Player of the Week 5:
Sean Hernandez – Miami Brewers – Complete Game Win and 3 Run Home Run!
Sean won a free hour at the batting cages courtesy of Hit Zone – Miami Tropical Park.

t: Player of the Week 1 – Greg Knoll – South Florida Beach Bums
b:
The South Florida Baseball League presents our Fall Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 1 – Greg Knoll – South Florida Bums, who went 4-4 with 3 doubles and 3 RBI and a run scored to lead the Bums to an 11-5 win over the Los Miami Dodgers.
Starting the new season off with a BANG, Greg Knoll, our Player of the Week 1!

Bases Loaded Batting Cages presents Week 1 player of the week:
(Photo pending)
Rafael Quintero
Broward Indians – 6 2/3 innings 0 ERA 12K, 3-3, RBI, 2 SB.
Rafael also has won 1 free hour of batting cage time at Bases Loaded Batting Cages.
Honorable mention:
Arias Garvi – Miramar Rangers – 3-4, HR, 2 RBI

t: Player of the Week 6 – Jorge Carpio – Dade Red Sox
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents our Spring Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 6 – Jorge Carpio – Dade Red Sox, who had a perfect day at the plate, going 3-3 with 3 RBI!
Sweet! No wonder the Dade Red Sox have been making waves.
Working on perfection every Sunday, Jorge Carpio, our Player of the Week 6!

t: Player of the Week 5 – David Galeno – Miramar Prospects
b:
The South Florida Baseball League presents our Spring Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 5 – David Galeno  – Miramar Prospects, who went 3-3, with a triple, double and a single and 4 RBI to power his team to a 7-6 win over the Miami Yankees.
Perfect at the plate, David Galeno, our Player of the Week 5!

t: Player of the Week 7 – Jay Warman – South Florida Rays
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents our Fall Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 7 – Jay Warman – South Florida Rays, who went 4-5 with a GRAND SLAM and a 2-run homer while collecting SIX RBI in a blowout 27-8 win over the Miami Red Sox.
A man playing among boys, Jay Warman, our Player of the Week 7!

Hit Zone Batting Cages presents Player of the Week 6:
Alex Marquez – South Florida Dodgers – Grand Slam 5 RBI
Alex won a free hour at the batting cages courtesy of Hit Zone – Miami Tropical Park.

Phone: (305) 448-9663

t: Player of the Week 9 – Makeel Rodgers – Aventura Dodgers
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents our Spring SeasonPLAYER OF THE WEEK 9 – Makeel Rodgers – Aventura Dodgers, who hit a walk off line drive to propel his Dodgers team to a tough win over the Miami Baystars.
Delivering with the game on the line, Makeel Rodgers, our Player of the Week 9!

Hit Zone Batting Cages presents Player of the Week 7:
Paul Proenza – Miami Red Sox – 3 RUN HR 5 RBI
Paul won a free hour at the batting cages courtesy of Hit Zone – Miami Tropical Park.

Hit Zone Batting Cages presents Players of the Week 10:
George Kenhan – Miami Brewers, starting pitcher and closer no runs allowed, and a homer to lead the Brewers over rival, Southern Yankees.
George won a free hour at the batting cages courtesy of Hit Zone – Miami Tropical Park.

t: Fall 2018 Player of the Week 8 – Luis Capriles – Valma Senators
b:
South Florida Baseball League presents Fall 2018 Season Player of the Week 8 – Luis Capriles – Valma Senators, who pitched a NO HITTER against the Miami Cardinals. One word – NASTY!
He loves his ZEROs, congratulations on earning our Player of the Week 8 honors – Luis Capriles!

t: Player of the Week 1 – Josue Bastera, Dade Nationals
b: The South Florida Baseball League presents our Fall Season PLAYER OF THE WEEK 1 – Josue Bastera, Dade Nationals, who went 4-5 with 3 doubles and 6 RBI. Wow – what a day at the plate! Congratulations to Josue for setting the tone for the season with this lead off performance.

t: Player of the Week 1 – Beach Bums Pitching Staff
b: The SFBL presents our PLAYER OF THE WEEK 1 – the wonderful combination of four Beach Bums pitchers, who pitched a combined NO HITTER versus the Dade Cardinals on Opening Day. Brian Steyer, Mason McLaren, Sal Grilli and Angel Morales – painting Picassos all day long!

t: Player of the Week 1 – South Florida Beach Bums Pitching Staff
b: The SFBL presents our PLAYER OF THE WEEK 1 – the wonderful combination of four Beach Bums pitchers, who pitched a combined NO HITTER versus the Dade Cardinals on Opening Day. Brian Steyer, Mason McLaren, Sal Grilli and Angel Morales – painting Picassos all day long!

Hit Zone Batting Cages presents Players of the Week 11:
Isaac Seoane – Coral Gables Athletics, 4-5, 2 runs, RBI to lead the A’s in big win over the Miami Rays.
Isaac won a free hour at the batting cages courtesy of Hit Zone – Miami Tropical Park.

Winter 1994 Season
History: By the fourth season, Winter 1994, the SFBL had established a presence in Miami, getting front page Sports Section coverage in the Miami Herald and making the cover of Hardball Magazine. A total of twenty four teams competed in the 14 game season, including 10 teams in the MSBL (30+) age bracket.
The El Dorado put together a fabulous season to win their second championship in four seasons, when they beat the Southern Bell Rangers 5 to 2. The Rangers upset the heavily favored former champions, Fountainbleu Angels in the semifinals to reach the championship while the Dodgers squeezed by a very tough Miami Marlins team which featured the Cy Young winner and strike-out king, Stefan Majer.
The Miami Giants win their first of many championships by coming from behind in the ninth inning of the championship game to edge the Miami Red Sox 8 to 7. Both teams earned the berth to the show by winning one run semifinal games.
