Tournament Summary
Florida Hardball presents the 2013 Florida Cup Baseball Tournament. This contest, which is open to any and all teams in the region regardless of affiliation, takes place in Miami, Florida on Labor Day weekend, August 31 – September 2, 2013. The tournament features some of the best competition in Florida with teams coming from various leagues including the South Florida Baseball League, Fort Myers Hardball, the Orlando – Central Florida Baseball League, the Federal League, Grapeland League, and the Liga Nica. The winner-take-all tournament is highlighted by a tournament trophy for the championship team and tournament medals and t-shirts for the players. The registration fee is $850 per team, regardless of player count. The deadline for team registration is Saturday, August 17, 2013.
Tournament Contact Number – 305.496.2962 – Mickey Filippucci
Tournament Teams:
1. Orlando Cardinals
2. Central Florida Vipers
3. Coral Gables A's
4. Dade Diamondbacks
5. South Florida Rays
6. Miami Red Sox
Tournament Format
The tournament will be limited to 6 – 8 teams using an open division WOOD BAT format (18 years of age and over). Teams will be seeded by the tournament director based on past experience, team history and other objective and subjective factors. Once seeding is complete, the tournament field will be broken down into two divisions. All teams are guaranteed a minimum of three games in the qualification round played against teams within their respective division. Two games are played on Saturday, August 31, 2013 with the third game played on Sunday morning, September 1, 2013.
The top two teams with the most points after the three game qualification round in each bracket will advance to the semi-final playoffs. All ties are resolved based on tie-breaker rules.
Playoff Qualifiers point system: Win = 2 points, Tie = 1 point, Loss = 0 points.
Tie Breaker Rules: 1) Head-to-head result; 2) Fewest Runs Allowed; 3) Net Runs (Runs Scored – Runs Allowed); 4) Coin Toss (best-of-three).
Playoffs: The semi-final games are scheduled to play Sunday afternoon, September 1, 2013. The winners advance to the Florida Cup Championship Monday morning September 2, 2013.
Tournament Rules (played under SFBL Official Rules): minimum 10 batters in the offensive line-up (may expand, not shrink); free defensive substitution except pitcher re-entry rule; pitcher re-entry only after one complete inning, once per game; no deliberate physical contact between base runners and defensive players (automatic ejection); qualification round games subject to a 3 hour time limit; in case of rain, official game recorded only after 5 full innings; managers must call in game results within 10 minutes.
Tournament Locations: Flamingo Park – Miami Beach; American High School – Hialeah; Curtis Park – Miami.

Welcome to SFBL FALL 2021 SEASON
Fall 2021 Season opens DECEMBER 12, 2021.  This will be our 58th consecutive season stretching 29 years of operation.
FALL 2021 Season highlights:
League Fees:  The players registration fee has been increased to $260 per player, payable prior to the start of the season.  Team fees are based on 13 paid rostered players, which cover the team registration fee as well as all umpire fees.  The math is as follows:  13 players * $260 = $3,380. The umpire fees for each team amount to 12 games * $90 = $1,080.  The team registration fee is $2,300.  The $1,080 umpire fees plus the $2,300 team fee adds up to exactly $3,380 (13 paid players).  The team registration fee is a flat fee.  All fees collected from extra players stay with the team should the team decide to carry more than 13 players on their roster.
Also, this season we are working on implementing a number of new initiatives designed to enhance your baseball experience, including the following:
Rolling 2 Week Schedule, where, with team compliance, we can provide all league participants enhanced forward planning capability. Team managers MUST disclose in advance all of their forward calendar conflicts so we can incorporate those conflicts into the scheduling process.  Once the schedule is posted, teams MUST either play or forfeit the game and pay both umpire fees in full.
Live streaming – GAME OF THE WEEK on the SFBL website and through your phone via Facebook
Automatic Roster Rollover, where only new players need to register. Once your players are rostered, we will be able to advise you promptly which of your players are not registered. Unregistered players are prohibited from playing in the league.
Online Store – initially we will be offering SFBL caps and t-shirts for sale online.
Finally, just a few reminders:
Team Waiver Form – download off the SFBL website and obtain the signatures of all rostered players. This protects the team managers and fellow players, the field operators and owners as well as the league from lawsuits.  Please understand the importance of this requirement.  Understand that if we do not have a player’s signature, he can’t play. PERIOD.
New Rules have been adopted. Please take the time to re-read the rule book.  Some of the new rules are of special interest, including the Playoff Tie Breaker Rule, which states that should the playoff game be tied after the official 3 hour time limit expires, then the game shall continue with each subsequent half inning starting with bases loaded and one out.
We are resuming Player of the Week and the Baseball Beast Award – for the most studly performance of the season as voted by your peers. Please submit nominations with photo.
Playoff Qualifications:  The top four teams in each division qualify for the post-season action.
Awards – The champions of each division (Open – Senior – Masters) earn the following awards:  Team Championship Trophy, MVP Trophy, Championship T-Shirts, authentic SFBL Logo Caps and a $1000 Cash Prize paid out at the field during the awards ceremony.
Managers Meetings:  The FALL 2021 Season Managers Meetings are scheduled for Saturday, December 11, 2021.  The Broward meeting will be held at NOON at HOOTERS at Pines and University in Pembroke Pines, while the Miami-DADE meeting will be held at 230PM at the Ale House on Bird Road and 67th Avenue in Miami.
South Miami – December 1, 2021

PLAYOFFS – SPRING 2019 Season concludes with post-season action NOV 3 – 17. The League will crown new champions in all three divisions: OPEN 18+ SENIOR 30+ and MASTERS 40+ by Thanksgiving!
FALL 2019 SEASON HIGHLIGHTS:
OPENING DAY is DECEMBER 8, 2019.
REGISTRATION FEES – $240 PER PLAYER PER SEASON
PLAYERS – NEW RULE – EACH PLAYER MUST REGISTER ONLINE TO BE ELIGIBLE TO PLAY – NO EXCEPTIONS.
ROSTERS – OFFICIAL NOTICE – SFBL WILL STRICTLY ENFORCE ROSTERS AND LIABILITY WAIVER FORMS. TEAMS IN VIOLATION WILL FORFEIT – NO EXCEPTIONS.
TEAM WAIVER FORM – Each team MUST submit a TEAM WAIVER FORM which includes the signatures of every player on the official roster – NO EXCEPTIONS. Form is available online.
ELIGIBLE PLAYERS POSTED ONLINE – LEAGUE WILL PUBLISH AND MAINTAIN ONLINE AN ALPHABETICAL LISTING OF ALL ELIGIBLE PLAYERS IN THE LEAGUE. IF A PLAYER IS NOT ON THE LIST, HE CAN’T PLAY. PERIOD.
PLAYOFF ELIGIBILITY – Reminder – to qualify to play in any playoff game, a player must play a minimum of three (3) games during the regular season.
BORROWED PLAYERS – MUST BE CLEARED AND APPROVED by the League prior to any play. Teams anticipating being short nine players for any particular ball game are requested to contact the League to arrange for a borrowed player.
UNIFORMS – Uniform code to be enforced after GAME 2. Players out of uniform after the second game of the season will be prohibited from participating in any official league games unless prior approval from the League is arranged.
NO ALCOHOL – STRICTLY ENFORCED AT EVERY FIELD!
DUGOUTS – Teams are required to clean up their DUGOUT after each game. VIOLATIONS WILL NOT BE TOLERATED.
UMPIRES – NEW – Effective FALL 2019 Season, teams will be responsible for ALL regular season umpire fees, including makeup games. Also, the SFBL Umpire Evaluation Form is now available online.
NEW RULES:
In order to play in an official league game, the player’s name MUST appear on the official LINE UP CARD submitted prior to the start of any game, even if the player is not present at the beginning of the game. Games will NOT start until both official lineups have been submitted to the plate umpire.
TEAMS ARE NO LONGER REQUIRED TO BAT 10 players. Teams may bat more than nine players and can even bat their entire lineup if they chose to; HOWEVER, teams cannot shrink the lineup without incurring an out first time around in the batting order.
Teams showing up to play with a minimum of 8 players may play an official game by borrowing a position player from the opposing team for defensive purposes only; however, the team batting with eight batters will receive an AUTOMATIC OUT EACH TIME that slot arrives in the batting order. The opposing team MUST provide the team short players with a position player throughout the game, again, for defensive purposes only.
Automatic runner for the catcher each time he reaches base safely. Use last batted out in the order.
Managers must notify the League on any injuries that occur during the game via an INJURY REPORT within 24 hours of any injury (an email with all relevant facts).
Teams are free to add players throughout the season; however, PLAYERS BEING ADDED AFTER OPENING DAY MUST BE APPROVED BY THE LEAGUE and must comply with all registration requirements to be eligible to play.

To join us this Spring 2005 Season please follow the registration instructions below.
You must first download Adobe Acrobat Reader and install it in order to view and print our League documents. The Team Registration Deadline for the Spring 2005 Season is Wednesday, February 16, 2005. Player Registration remains open indefinitely.
Player Registration:
1) To register as an individual player, simply print out and complete the Player Registration Form and mail it in along with your payment. Cash or checks (payable to SFBL) in the amount of $220 are accepted. For your convenience, all major credit cards ($229) are accepted as well.
2) Once your registration form is received you will be placed into the SFBL Player Pool. You can confirm your status by visiting the SFBL Player Pool Link, which is updated daily. Players are then transferred onto teams based on age, position and city of residence.
3) Once you have been assigned to a team, your team manager will contact you to welcome you aboard and to review questions regarding team uniforms, team practices, etc.
Team Registration:
1) To register a team, managers must first contact the league office via email to have their team officially added to the Spring 2005 Season League Line-up. After the team is added, a User ID and temporary password will be assigned to the manager and/or his assistant.
2) Using the User ID and password, managers must then load their team rosters into the SFBL database via the Login screen. After logging in, use the ROSTER function key to access the team roster. Build the roster using the NEW ROW function key to add individual player information (mailing address, DOB, phone numbers & position) and SAVE after each player. Before exiting, change the temporary password to a unique password.
3) Print out Team Waiver Form and have each player on roster sign and date it. Managers must submit the completed Team Waiver Form to the League Office by registration deadline.
4) Managers must submit player registration fees to the League Office by registration deadline. The minimum team fee is $2,860, based on a minimum 13-man roster @ $220 per player. The team fee increases by $220 for each additional player over the minimum. Cash, checks (payable to SFBL) and major credit cards are accepted.
5) In addition to player registration fees, managers must submit a $100 cash deposit to secure the team's ongoing responsibilities to the league; namely, ensuring all player statistics and game results are properly recorded into the SFBL database weekly, and no forfeits take place. Teams with perfect attendance on field (no forfeits) and off field (weekly stats and scores) will be awarded a full refund of the cash deposit. Teams with deficient weekly stats will be ineligible for post season play.
6) The one and only managers meeting for this season is scheduled for 8:00 pm Wednesday, March 2, 2005 at Don Shula's Perfect Season Meeting Room in Miami Lakes. In addition to participating in several manager votes, you will receive three boxes of MSBL logo baseballs, schedules and scorebooks.

Press Release
South Miami, December 18, 2016
The new Fall 2016 season opened on December 18, 2016 with a game between the Miami Brewers and the Hialeah Astros (Brewers won 8-2). This is our 49th consecutive season and baseball in the South Florida Baseball League is more exciting than ever. To keep things simple, we are keeping all the rules the same, with the exception of bats, where the SFBL has formally adopted the COLLEGE BASEBALL BAT RULES for both OPEN AND MASTERS DIVISIONS.
This season, for each division, Open and Masters, the SFBL is awarding Championship and MVP trophies, a $1000 cash prize, championship tshirts and authentic SFBL logo baseball caps to the championship teams.  The SFBL is also awarding the Baseball Beast trophy to our most heroic warrior.
All games, except championship games, are subject to 3 hour time limit, no ties permitted, suspended after 3.5 hours, mercy rule 10 run lead after seven innings in effect.  Championship games do not have a time limit.  All games are official after five complete innings if called due to rain.
Special Note:  The $1000 cash prize will only be payable to championship teams in good standing, meaning teams must be in full compliance with a published team roster, matching waiver form signatures, fully paid team fees and full team statistics in order to collect the incentive cash prize.
Open Division Playoffs:
The Open Division will consist of 12 teams for the new season.  The top six teams at the end of the season are seeded 1 through 6 to compete in the playoffs:
Quarter-finals:
Top two seeds each gets a BYE with an automatic entry into the semi-finals.
Quarter-finals Game 1:  The 6 seed (Visitors) plays the 3 seed (Home team)
Quarter-finals Game 2:  The 5 seed (Visitors) plays the 4 seed (Home team)
Semi-finals:
Winner of the Quarter-finals Game 2 (Visitors) plays the 1 seed (Home team), while the winner of the Quarter-finals Game 1 (Visitors) plays the 2 seed (Home team).
Championship:
The winners of the semi-final games play a championship game with the team with the best overall record (with tie breakers, if necessary) earning the home team designation.
Masters Division Playoffs:
For Fall 2016 Season, the SFBL will field six teams in the Masters Division (ages 30+).  At the end of the season, the top three teams in the division will qualify to compete in the playoffs for a $1000 cash prize, Championship and MVP trophies, championship tshirts and authentic SFBL logo baseball caps.
The 30+ Masters Division teams must carry a roster of players turning 30 and older in the current year, with three exceptions. Masters Divisions teams are permitted to carry up to three players turning 27 and older in the current year, regardless of position played.
Semi-finals:
The Number 1 seed earns an automatic Bye into the Championship game.
The Number 3 seed (Visitors) plays the Number 2 seed (Home team).
Championship:
The winner of the semi-final game plays the top seed in a one game championship game for all the marbles and the greater glory.

DUGOUTS – Teams are required to clean up their DUGOUT after each game. VIOLATIONS WILL NOT BE TOLERATED.

Press Release
South Miami, November 14, 2015
The new season brings a slew of exciting changes for the South Florida Baseball League. In particular, we have simplified the league and made it more exciting than ever. Effective the Spring 2015 Season, the SFBL now operates two divisions – Open Division (ages 18+) and Masters Division (ages 30+). The League uses aluminum bats but wood bats are permitted.
The SFBL is awarding a championship trophy, championship T-shirts and a $1000 cash bonus to the championship team of each division. The SFBL will award an MVP trophy for each division. Finally, the SFBL will award the very special Baseball Beast trophy to our most heroic warrior.
Special Note: The $1000 cash prize will only be payable to championship teams in good standing, meaning teams must be in full compliance with a published team roster, matching waiver form signatures, fully paid team fees and full team statistics in order to collect the incentive cash bonus.
PLAYOFF POLICY:
All games, except championship games, are subject to 3 hour time limit, no ties permitted. Games are suspended after 3.5 hours. No inning will start with less than 5 minutes remaining. Mercy Rule – 10 run lead after seven innings in effect. Championship games do not have a time limit. ALL games, including championship games, are official after five complete innings if called due to rain.
OPEN DIVISION:
The top six teams at the end of the season qualify for the Post Season.
Quarter-finals: Number 1 seed in each bracket gets a BYE with an automatic entry into the semi-finals. The Number 3 team (Visitors) plays the Number 2 team (Home team) with the winner advancing to the semi-finals in both brackets.
Semi-finals: Winner of the quarter-finals (Visitors) plays the Number 1 seed (Home team) in both brackets.
Championship: The winners of the semi-final games play the championship game, with the team with the best regular season record earning the home team designation. A coin flip will break a tie.
Masters Division Playoffs:
The 30+ Masters Division teams must carry a roster of players turning 30 and older in the current year, with three exceptions. Masters Divisions teams are permitted to carry up to three players turning 27 and older in the current year, regardless of position played. The top team in each bracket and two teams with the next best record (points) at the end of the season qualify for the Post Season.
Semi-finals: The Number 4 seed (Visitors) plays the Number 1 seed (Home team) and the Number 3 seed (Visitors) plays the Number 2 seed (Home).
Championship: The winners of the semi-final games play the championship game, with the best overall record earning the home team designation. A coin flip will break a tie.

The South Florida Baseball League is proud to announce the inception of the 2002 Summer League that will commence in July 2002.  After getting much interest from the St. Thomas University Baseball Program, the League Office was able to finalize a format to allow for baseball to continue play on Sundays between the Spring and Fall Seasons.
The league will consist of 8 teams playing 8 games in 8 weeks in semi-college competition.  Besides the St. Thomas University J.V. team, 4 other teams have confirmed participation this summer, with 3 other teams being invited to play.  One of the teams that have confirmed are the Santa Fe Reds, which are a semi-professional team out of the West Palm Beach area.  From the SFBL, the South Florida Mets out of the Premier division have confirmed participation, as well the Miami Aqua Sox, a combination of several MSBL players, and Team Miami, a combination of the Coral Gables Athletics and Miami Devil Rays.  The other teams participating will be announced later this week.
Team Fee: $1300 flat fee per team.
Fields: The fields being utilized for the Summer League will be St. Thomas University, Coral Gables High, and Flamingo Park on South Beach.
Rules: SFBL rules will be applied.
Divisions and Playoffs: The 8 teams will be divided into the National and American Divisions.  The top 2 teams in each division will make the playoffs with a 2 round playoff system to determine the champion.  In the first round, the first place team in the National Division will play the second place team in the American Division and the first place team in the American Division will play the second place in the National Division.  The Championship Game will consist of the winners of the first round games squaring off against each other.
American Division
National Division
St. Thomas University A

Coral Gables Athletics
Santa Fe Reds
Miami Diamondbacks
South Florida Mets
Miami Aqua Sox
Broward Braves
American Amateur Baseball Congress: Another unique feature of the 2002 Summer League is the SFBL's affiliation with the AABC.  The affiliation provides the Championship Team of the league to participate in the AABC's State Tournament which will be held in late July in St. Petersburg, FL.  Similar state tournaments will be held in Tennessee, Kentucky, North Carolina, South Carolina, Alabama, and Georgia.  The championship team from each state tournament will then go on to participate in the National Tournament which will be in the Fort Lauderdale Stadium.
Interested Players:  Players who are interested in playing in the Summer League should contact the League office as soon as possible.
Base Hits as of 10/2/02

t: Orlando Madness wins 2015 MIAMI OPEN over Miami Red Sox
b:
The Orlando Madness baseball club win 3rd Trophy in Miami, beating the Miami Red Sox 9-1 in the finals of the 2015 Miami Open Championship!
Picture this, Memorial Day Holiday Weekend, South Beach Flamingo Baseball Stadium, spectacular weather, and it’s Miami versus Orlando in the opening game of the 2015 Miami Open Baseball Tournament. Baseball Heaven!
Well, the opening game of the tournament went to the Miami Red Sox, who came from behind to beat the Orlando Madness team 6-5 on a walk-off RBI single by Bo Mulford in the bottom of the tenth inning. And it was another great game by the crafty veteran, John Emmons, who pitched all ten innings to pick up another big tournament game win. Emmons held the Orlando team in check for the entire game, giving up only a few earned runs. The Orlando team was down 4-0 but chipped away at the deficit each inning until a two run bomb to right field put them on top 5-4 after seven. But the Miami team tied the score on a pair of Jimmy Roberts crazy legs stolen bases before Mulford won it in the bottom of the tenth with a little magic stroke.
Orlando came back in the second game, beating the Fort Lauderdale Sting Rays 6-1 behind solid pitching and timely hitting. The late game featured a Fort Lauderdale – Miami matchup, with the Miami Red Sox pounding out a 8-1 lead. But the Sting Rays would not go down without a fight and rallied to put the game on the line in the ninth before losing 10-8. What a thriller.
Day 2 of the tournament featured the semifinal game in the morning with the Fort Lauderdale Sting Rays taking on the Orlando Madness. Orlando broke on top early 2-0 and then slowly pulled away before the inning from hell buried the Rays. Final score 9-1, Orlando over Fort Lauderdale. So the Orlando club advanced to the championship game versus the undefeated Miami Red Sox. And the rematch was on, but then, suddenly, Orlando was up and running away with the game on defensive lapses by the Sox. In the end, the Orlando team collected their third trophy in their semi-annual South Florida Hunt. Good hunting Orlando Madness. Well deserved!
Notice Posted May 13:
The 2015 Miami Open will be held over the Memorial Day Weekend at Flamingo Park on South Beach. The four team bash starts at 9am Saturday May 23 with Orlando Madness taking on hometown Miami Red Sox in the tournament opener. The three game round robin tournament culminates with semifinal and championship games on Monday May 25.
Tournament teams are Orlando Madness, Miami Reds, Fort Lauderdale Sting Rays and Miami Red Sox.
Players interested in participating in the tournament should call the league office asap 305.496.2962.

Tournament Summary
Florida Hardball presents the 2014 Florida Cup Baseball Tournament. This contest, which is open to any and all teams in the region regardless of affiliation, takes place in Miami, Florida on Labor Day weekend, August 30 – September 1, 2014. The tournament features some of the best competition in Florida with teams coming from various leagues including the South Florida Baseball League, Fort Myers Baseball League, the Orlando – Central Florida Baseball League, the Federal League, Grapeland League, and the Liga Nica. The winner-take-all tournament is highlighted by a tournament trophy for the championship team, tournament t-shirts for the players and a spot in the record books. The registration fee is $850 per team, with a maximum 20 man roster limit. The deadline for team registration is August 20, 2014.
Tournament Contact Number – 305.496.2962 – Mickey Filippucci
Tournament Format
The tournament will be limited to 6 – 8 teams using an open division WOOD BAT format (18 years of age and over). Teams will be seeded by the tournament director. Once seeding is complete, the field will be broken down into two divisions. All teams are guaranteed a minimum of three games in the qualification round played against teams within their respective division. Two games are played on Saturday, August 30, 2014 with the third game played on Sunday morning, August 31, 2014.
The top two teams with the most points after the three game qualification round in each bracket will advance to the semi-final playoffs.
Playoff Qualifiers point system: Win = 2 points, Tie = 1 point, Loss = 0 points.
Tie Breaker Rules: 1) Head-to-head result; 2) Fewest Runs Allowed; 3) Net Runs (Runs Scored – Runs Allowed); 4) Coin Toss (best-of-three).
Playoffs: The semi-final games are scheduled to play Sunday afternoon, August 31, 2014. The winners advance to the Florida Cup Championship Monday morning September 1, 2014.
Tournament Rules (played under SFBL Official Rules): minimum 10 batters in the offensive line-up (may expand, not shrink); free defensive substitution except pitcher re-entry rule; pitcher re-entry only after one complete inning, once per game; no deliberate physical contact between base runners and defensive players (automatic ejection); qualification round games subject to a 3 hour time limit; in case of rain, official game recorded only after 5 full innings; managers must call in game results within 10 minutes.
Tournament Locations: Flamingo Park – Miami Beach; West Perrine Park – Miami; Charles Hadley Park – Miami; other fields to be determined.

Tournament Summary
The Independence Holiday Classic is open to any and all teams in the region regardless of affiliation and takes place in Miami, Florida on July 4th weekend (July 5-7, 2013). The tournament features some of the best competition in Florida with teams coming from various leagues including The Grapeland League, South Florida Baseball League, Fort Myers Hardball, the Orlando – Central Florida Baseball League, the Federal League, NABA and The Hialeah League. The winner-take-all tournament is highlighted by a tournament trophy for the championship team and tournament medals and t-shirts for the players. The registration fee is $850 per team, regardless of player count. The deadline for team registration is Wednesday June 26, 2013.
Tournament Contact Number – (305) 439-2644 – Jay Habacht
Teams tentatively scheduled to participate: Miami A's (2013 Miami Open Champions), Miami Red Sox, Dade Diamondbacks, Central Florida (Orlando) Padres, Grapeland Pirates.
Tournament Format
The tournament will be an open division wood bat format (18+). All teams are guaranteed a minimum of three games in the qualification round played against teams within their respective division. Two games are played on Friday July 5, 2013 with the third game played on Saturday morning July 6, 2013.
The top team will automatically play in the finals on Sunday July 7, 2013. The #2 and #3 teams will play in the semi-final round Saturday July 6, 2013 in the afternoon. The winner of this game will face the top seed in the finals on Sunday July 7, 2013.
Playoff Qualifiers point system: Win = 2 points, Tie = 1 point, Loss = 0 points.
Tie Breaker Rules: 1) Head-to-head result; 2) Fewest Runs Allowed; 3) Net Runs (Runs Scored – Runs Allowed); 4) Coin Toss (best-of-three).
Playoffs: The semi-final games are scheduled to play Saturday afternoon, July 6, 2013. The winners advance to the Independence Holiday Classic Championship Sunday morning July 7, 2013.
Basic Tournament Rules: minimum 10 batters in the offensive line-up (may expand, not shrink); free defensive substitution except pitcher re-entry rule; pitcher re-entry only after one complete inning, once per game; no deliberate physical contact between base runners and defensive players (automatic ejection); qualification round games subject to a 3 hour time limit; in case of rain, official game recorded only after 5 full innings; managers must call in game results within 10 minutes to (305) 439-2644.
Tournament Locations: Curtis Park; Coral Gables High School; Florida Memorial University.

Tournament Summary
The South Florida Baseball League presents the 11th Annual South Beach Slam Tournament. This contest, which is open to any and all teams in the region regardless of affiliation, takes place in Miami, Florida on July 4th weekend, July 5-7, 2013. The tournament features some of the best competition in Florida with teams coming from various leagues including the South Florida Baseball League, Los Amigos del Grapeland, The Hialeah League, Fort Myers Hardball, the Orlando – Central Florida Baseball League, the Federal League and La Liga Nica. The winner-take-all tournament is highlighted by a tournament trophy for the championship team and tournament medals and t-shirts for the players. The registration fee is $850 per team, regardless of player count. The deadline for team registration is Saturday June 29, 2013.
Tournament Contact Number – 305.496.2962 – Mickey Filippucci
Tournament Format
The tournament will be an open division wood bat format (18+). All teams are guaranteed a minimum of three games in the qualification round played against teams within their respective division. Two games are played on Friday July 5, 2013 with the third game played on Saturday morning, July 6, 2013.
The top team will automatically play in the finals on Sunday July 7, 2013. The #2 and #3 teams will play in the semi-final round Saturday July 6, 2013 in the afternoon. The winner of this game will face the top seed in the finals on Sunday July 7, 2013.
Playoff Qualifiers point system: Win = 2 points, Tie = 1 point, Loss = 0 points.
Tie Breaker Rules: 1) Head-to-head result; 2) Fewest Runs Allowed; 3) Net Runs (Runs Scored – Runs Allowed); 4) Coin Toss (best-of-three).
Playoffs: The semi-final games are scheduled to play Saturday afternoon, July 6, 2013. The winners advance to the South Beach Slam Championship Sunday morning July 7, 2013.
Tournament Rules (played under SFBL Official Rules): minimum 10 batters in the offensive line-up (may expand, not shrink); free defensive substitution except pitcher re-entry rule; pitcher re-entry only after one complete inning, once per game; no deliberate physical contact between base runners and defensive players (automatic ejection); qualification round games subject to a 3 hour time limit; in case of rain, official game recorded only after 5 full innings; managers must call in game results within 10 minutes.
Tournament Locations: Flamingo Park – Miami Beach; Coral Gables High – Coral Gables; Curtis Park – Miami.

Photo of Masters and Senior Division All Stars – January 11, 2015 – Cypress Bay Vista Park Weston FL. The South Florida Baseball League held the Fall 2014 season ALL STAR GAMES this past Sunday, January 11, 2015 at the beautiful Cypress Bay Vista Park in Weston. The morning game featured the Masters Division All Stars against the Senior Division Team 2 All Stars. The second game featuring the Premier Division All Stars and the Senior Division Team 1 All Stars was rained out and will be made up in the Spring.
But the game was a BLAST! The Senior Division All Stars defeated the Masters Division All Stars 9-3. Joe Celli – Broward Indians started for the Senior All Stars and pitched three scoreless innings against the Masters Division All Stars, using mainly off speed pitching. Carlos Torres – Florida Yankees was almost equally as effective for the Masters team, holding the Senior team to just one run over four innings. However, the Seniors strung together 5 singles in the 5th inning to break the game open, breaking a 1-1 tie with a five run inning. The Senior All Stars were managed by Manny Fernandez-Deerfield White Sox, while the Masters All Stars were skippered by Jorge Orberta Sr.-Dade Nationals.
The defensive players of the game were Masters team Matt Goodnow-Miramar Rangers at third, making play after play, and the Senior team Howard Capote-Miami Cardinals, who ran down countless deep flies in center field. Offensively, the Seniors were led by Jay Warman-South Florida Sting Rays, 2-3, 3 RBI, Howard Capote-Miami Cardinals, solo HR, Richard Perez-Miami Dodgers, 2-3, 2 RBI, and Juan Machado-Deerfield White Sox, 3-3.
The Masters team was led by Jesus Delgado-Miramar Rangers, 2-3 and Matt Goodnow-Miramar Rangers, 2-4, 2B. However, we want to give Carlos Pandolfo– South Florida Dodgers a special shout out. After flying out to the warning track in deep center field on back to back trips to the plate, Carlos drilled an RBI single in his third and final at bat. The man crushed the ball all day.
But the biggest BLAST OF THE GAME was Howard Capote’s moon shot over the tall center field fence. It was an amazing display of raw power! GO HOWARD!
And then it came down to electing the MOST VALUABLE PLAYER – JAY WARMAN – SOUTH FLORIDA STING RAYS. The 3 RBI in his first two trips to the plate set the tone for the game and the Senior Team never looked back. Congratulations Jay Warman – our 2015 SFBL ALL STAR MVP!

t: Fall 2007 Season to Launch on Sunday, September 23, 2007 — Managers must load rosters asap
b:
The South Florida Baseball League (SFBL) operates under the umbrella of Florida Hardball, Inc., one of the premier adult baseball organizations in Florida, covering Dade, Monroe, Broward and Palm Beach counties. Opening day for the Fall 2007 season is September 23, 2007 — the league's 16th year and 31st consecutive season. The SFBL caters to men 18 and older and anticipates fielding 36 or more teams with over 500 active players.
Florida Hardball also operates a sister league, Fort Myers Hardball, on the West Coast (Naples, Fort Myers, Cape Coral, Port Charlotte). The Florida Hardball Championship at each season's end pits the SFBL Premier Division East Coast champion against the West Coast champion.
Schedule: two regular seasons per year (Spring and Fall) plus two statewide tournaments, the Miami Open (Memorial Day Weekend) and the Florida Cup (Labor Day Weekend). All games are played on Sundays, each season 12 regular games plus playoffs and a championship for each age bracket.
Fees: $220 per player, payable by cash, check or major credit card. Fees cover field rentals, umpire services, Rawlings R100 baseballs, liability insurance, website hosting, custom real-time statistics software and Leader Boards, tournament operations, MSBL national tournament registration, advertising, annual tryouts and live draft, and awards banquets. Uniform costs ($20-$75, typically Majestic pro-style replica jerseys) are not covered.
Age Divisions: four brackets — Premier 18+, Adult 18+, Senior 25+ and Masters 35+. Fields include Barry University, Homestead Baseball Complex, Miami Dade College, Flamingo Park, Broward Community College, Florida Memorial College, Lynn University and Sunset Park in Plantation. Officiated by the South Florida College Baseball Umpires Association under the Florida Hardball League Rules.
Extras include the Baseball Buzz weekly online newsletter, Coaches Poll, Player of the Week, Scouting Report, and publishing of scores in the Miami Herald and Sun Sentinel. Each season the SFBL hosts an awards banquet honoring the championship team and division MVPs. Contact the League Office at 305-663-2521. — Mickey Filippucci.

SOUTH FLORIDA BASEBALL LEAGUE
ABOUT SFBL
The South Florida Baseball League (SFBL) is one of the premier adult baseball organizations in the great state of Florida operating in Dade, Broward and Palm Beach counties. The SFBL caters to adult men of all ages who crave baseball, offering competition in three divisions; Open 18+, Senior 30+ and Masters 40+. The SFBL is a WOOD BAT LEAGUE. Considered by many to be one of the top adult amateur hardball organizations in the country, the SFBL provides high quality regional baseball competition in South Florida, including league play, all star games and tournaments.
All games are played on Sundays. The Spring Season and the Fall Season both consist of identical 12 regular season games as well as a series of postseason games, which include Quarterfinals, Semifinals and a Championship for each division. Winners earn a team Championship trophy, an MVP trophy and championship t-shirts and authentic SFBL logo caps for the players. Open and Senior Division Champions also win a $1000 CASH PRIZE!
The Opening Day for the Fall 2020 Season is December 13, 2020. This will be our 28th year of operation and our 56th consecutive season of baseball! New players and teams are encouraged to join the league right now and be a part of this special milestone.

Tournament Teams: 1. Miami Red Sox, 2. Miami Bull Dogs, 3. Broward Cardinals, 4. Miami AquaSox.
Schedule: The Miami Open Tournament schedule is posted on the website under the Schedules drop-down tab. To view tournament scores and standings, use the drop-down tabs and select "tournament" and "open division" options.
Tournament Format: The tournament is played in an open division format (18+). All teams are guaranteed a minimum of three games in round robin format against teams within their respective division. Two games are played Saturday, May 26 with the third game Sunday morning, May 27.
Playoffs: The first place team after the 3-game elimination round earns a bye and automatically advances to the championship game. The second and third place teams play a semi-final game Sunday afternoon May 27, with the winner advancing to play the first place team in the championship game, scheduled for 10am Monday May 28. All ties resolved by tie-breaker rules.
Playoff Qualifiers point system: Win = 2 points, Tie = 1 point, Loss = 0 points.
Tie Breaker Rules (in order): 1. Head-to-head result. 2. Fewest Runs Allowed. 3. Net Runs = (Runs Scored - Runs Allowed). 4. Best-of-three coin toss.
Tournament Rules: Played under the rules of the South Florida Baseball League. Key rules: (1) Minimum 10 batters in the offensive line-up; may add but not shrink once expanded. (2) Free defensive substitution except pitcher re-entry rule. (3) Pitcher re-entry: a removed pitcher may only re-enter as pitcher after one complete inning, once per game. (4) No deliberate physical contact between base runners and defensive players (automatic ejection). (5) Elimination round games subject to 3-hour time limit (no inning starts after 3 hours); ties permitted in round robin; semi-final games have 3-hour limit but no ties; championship game has no time limit. (6) In case of rain, official game only after 5 full innings; suspended games resume where they left off with frozen line-ups. (7) All managers must call in game results to the SFBL office within 30 minutes of game completion or suspension.

t: NEW SEASON MEMO TO TEAM MANAGERS
b:
Welcome to SFBL SPRING 2021 SEASON
Thank you for your interest in the South Florida Baseball League. We begin a new season with many upgrades designed to enhance your baseball experience. Specifically, we are introducing a number of new initiatives, including:
- A Rolling 2 Week Schedule, where, with team compliance, we can provide all league participants enhanced forward planning capability. Team managers MUST disclose in advance all of their forward calendar conflicts so we can incorporate those conflicts into the scheduling process. Once the schedule is posted, teams MUST play or forfeit and be suspended. A forfeit fee of $200 MUST BE PAID PRIOR TO REINSTATEMENT.
- Live streaming – GAME OF THE WEEK on the SFBL website and through your phone via Facebook.
- Automatic Roster Rollover, where only new players need to register. Once your players are rostered, we will be able to advise you promptly which of your players are not registered. Unregistered players are prohibited from playing in the league.
- Team Waiver Form – download off the SFBL website and obtain the signatures of all rostered players. This protects the team managers and fellow players, the field operators and owners as well as the league from lawsuits. Understand that if we do not have a player's signature, he can't play. PERIOD.
- New Rules have been adopted. Please take the time to re-read the rule book. Some of the new rules are of special interest, including the Playoff Tie Breaker Rule, which starts the 10th inning with based loaded and one out.
- We are resuming Player of the Week and the Baseball Beast Award – for the most studly performance of the season as voted by your peers. Please submit nominations with photo.
- Playoff Qualifications: OPEN DIV – top four in each bracket / SENIOR DIV top four / MASTERS DIV top three.
- Cash Prize and championship awards remain the same – Team Championship Trophy, MVP Trophy, Championship T-Shirts, authentic SFBL logo caps and a $1000 Cash Prize for Open and Senior Divisions and $500 Cash Prize for Masters Division.
June 1, 2021

The South Florida Baseball League (SFBL) is one of the premier adult baseball organizations in Florida, covering southeast Florida and operating in Dade, Broward and Palm Beach counties. It caters to adult men of all ages, offering competition in three divisions: Premier (18+) Wood Bat, Senior (18+) Aluminum and Masters (35+) Aluminum.
Opening Day for the Fall 2014 Season is November 9, 2014 — the league's 23rd year of operation and 45th consecutive season of baseball. All games are played on Sundays. The Spring and Fall seasons each consist of 12 regular season games plus postseason quarterfinals, semifinals and a best-of-three championship series for each division.
The SFBL operates two regular seasons per year (Spring and Fall) and two statewide tournaments: the Miami Open (Memorial Day Weekend) and the Florida Cup (Labor Day Weekend), drawing visiting teams from New York, Orlando, Jacksonville, Fort Myers, Tampa and Palm Beach. It reintroduced an All Star Sunday featuring two All Star games in cross-division matchups.
Membership fees are $230 per player. New players are assigned to the SFBL Player Pool and reassigned to teams based on team needs, age, position and city of residence. Fees cover field rentals and maintenance, umpire services (South Florida College Baseball Umpires Association), baseballs, liability insurance, website hosting, custom real-time statistics software and Leader Boards, tournament operations, All Star games, trophies and T-shirts, advertising, and league administration. Fees do not cover uniform costs ($20-$75).
Over 44 seasons the league has established parity via a live draft and player pool assignments; skill levels range from ex-minor leaguers to former softball players. Fields include Lynn University in Boca Raton and Flamingo Park on South Beach. The league enforces the Florida Hardball League Rules and promotes a wholesome, family, competitive environment. Championship trophies, T-shirts and an MVP award are presented for each division. Contact the League Office at 305.496.2962. — Mickey Filippucci.

Congratulations to The 2013 Florida Cup champions Orlando Cardinals!! The Cardinals played solid baseball against excellent competition throughout the tournament. The Cardinals won with top notch pitching, solid defense, timely hitting, and tremendous speed on the basepaths.
The Cardinals went a perfect 5-0 over Labor Day weekend. Their 1st game was against perennial tournament champions Coral Gables A’s, whom they beat 7-4. Their 2nd game vs The Miami Red Sox ended in a solid victory 10-3. On day 2, The Cards faced The Dade Diamondbacks and won 4-0, featuring solid pitching and defense. The Cardinals would again face The Diamondbacks in the 1st round of the playoffs and came out with a 15-5 win to advance to the championship game. The Cards would face the A’s again in an epic battle that would go down to the last pitch. The A’s would leave bases loaded in the 9th inning for an 8-7 victory and The Florida Cup championship.
Championship – 9/2/2013: Coral Gables Athletics 7, Orlando Florida Cardinals 8. 'A’s leave bases loaded in 9th' / 'Cardinals survive Athletics comeback' – Sloppy base running hurt the A’s early but the bats kept them in the game as they rallied from being down 8-2. The game came down to the last pitch as the A’s had the bases loaded with two out before the final out landed in the outfielder’s glove; the Orlando Cardinals hung on to squeak out the win.
Playoffs – 9/1/2013: Central Florida Vipers 6, Coral Gables Athletics 11 (A’s advance to Championship). Dade Diamondbacks 5, Orlando Florida Cardinals 15 (Cardinals advance to finals) – Diamondbacks Chris Rivero pitched well, giving up 1 earned run in the first 2 innings; Jay Habacht also pitched well, giving up only 1 earned run in 4 innings. Dade Diamondbacks vs Orlando Florida Cardinals 0-4 (Cards advance; Dbacks advanced to Semi-finals on tie breaker) – Diamondbacks Matt Leban records 1 of his 7 strike outs; Ivan Asension hits a double in the 3rd inning. Miami Red Sox 7, Central Florida Vipers (bracket play).

This History Page allows visitors to the website to experience each facet of the history of the South Florida Baseball League, featuring links to 25 seasons of SFBL history. Additional links include Championship History, Team History, SFBL Record Book and Top Ten History pages. Finally, coming soon will be the South Florida Baseball League Hall of Fame web page.
All History Pages Updated on 3/31/08 through Fall 2007 Season.
Season History: Provides an individual web page for each season in the history of the SFBL. Each page features a season summary, including play-off and championship scores, final regular season standings and final leader boards.
Championship History: Provides a summary of all championship games played in the 23 seasons of the SFBL.
Team History: Provides links to individual teams with a significant history in the SFBL. Team background information is listed as well as team links, if available. The highlight of these pages are tables featuring individual season records with playoff appearances and results. At the bottom of these tables, team stats are provided with overall records, winning percentages, playoff appearances, playoff records as well as other team trivia.
Record Book: Sub-divided into two record books, a Team Record Book and a Player Record Book. Team Record categories include most games, most wins, most losses, most playoff appearances, most championships, etc. Individual Player Records include all the Leaderboards categories as well as other records recorded over all the years in the SFBL.
Top Ten History: Features the top ten teams and players in each of the categories of the Record Book.
Hall of Fame: The history page will also feature a Hall of Fame. Inaugural members of the Hall of Fame will be announced shortly.
Player Awards: The League Office designed this page to establish the player awards and comps that are provided every season.

The South Florida Baseball League presents the Fall 2017 Season League Lineup featuring 30 awesome teams.
Open Division (18+) consists of 16 teams divided into two brackets Dade and Broward/Palm Beach. The top three teams in each bracket advance to the SFBL Playoffs with the first place team earning a BYE while the second and third seeded teams play the Quarterfinals. Then the top seed plays the Quarterfinals winner in each bracket in the Semifinals. Finally, the winners of the Semifinals advance to the Championship game for all the marbles.
Masters Division (30+) consists of 14 teams divided into two brackets of 6 teams. The top three teams in each bracket advance to the SFBL Playoffs with the first place team earning a BYE while the second and third seeded teams play the Quarterfinals. Then the top seed plays the Quarterfinals winner in each bracket in the Semifinals. Finally, the winners of the Semifinals advance to the Championship game for all the marbles.
The marbles... The winning teams in each division earn a Championship Trophy, an MVP Trophy, and championship t-shirts, authentic SFBL logo baseball caps and a $1,000 CASH PRIZE for the players! Let the big dog eat!
South Florida Baseball League Fall 2017 Season
Open Division 18+: Broward American, Dade National, Aventura Dodgers, Flomo Crusaders, Broward White Sox, Los Miami Dodgers, Davie Tigers, Miami Brewers, Delray Nationals, Miami Pirates, Fort Lauderdale Yankees, Miami Red Sox, Miami BayStars, Miramar Prospects, South Florida Beach Bums, South Florida Marlins, Weston Leones, South Florida Rays.
Masters Division 30+ — American: Broward Indians, Florida Yankees, Miramar Rangers, Palm Beach Angels, South Florida Dodgers, Southern Yankees, South Florida Twins. National: Dade Nationals, Dade Red Sox, Kendall Metz, Miami Cardinals, Miami Hurricanes, Miami Mariners, Miami Reds.

After 5 weeks in the Fall 2014 Season, we now can take a look at the standings and try to figure things out.
In the Premier Division, it looks like we are extremely balanced with the four wood bat teams sitting on 5 and 6 points in the standing. The Miami Red Sox lost their first game to perennial power Coral Gables Athletics, despite starting A’s killer Cory Casuso, and the Perrine Monarchs have yet to lose. Meanwhile the Miami Devil Dogs climbed back into contention.
In the Senior Division, the talk is all about can the Davie Tigers go back-to-back in Senior Championships. The Tigers are 3-0 in the Broward Bracket and are riding like Bengals in the Wild. Can the South Florida Beach Bums close the gap? Their disappointing start has left the team 2-2. The Bums were top pick on some polls. Can the Southern Yankees make a run? They are 2-2 as well. Newcomers, Westchester Athletics are looking good at 3-1, sitting on top of the Dade Bracket, with the Miami Brewers, Miami Cardinals and South Florida Sting Rays not living up to expectations. All three of those teams are expected to make the playoffs but sport unusually mediocre records.
The Masters Division looks like a one horse race at the moment with the defending champions, Palm Beach Angels, laying down a scorch the earth policy. The Miramar Rangers, were running with the Angels at the top of the heap until they ran into the Angels and got blown out. Show up with key guys missing and you will pay. Hold on, despite also getting blown out by the Angels, the Dade Nationals have quietly won three games and the South Florida Dodgers have won two. Make it into the playoffs and anything can happen. We have witnessed many a 12-0 or 11-1 team lose in the first round of the playoffs. It happens!
Coral Gables Athletics batter taking it to the Miami Devil Dogs.

t: SFBL looking for players for Palm Beach Classic – Roger Dean Stadium April 9-12
b:
The SFBL is looking to put a team into the 12th Annual Palm Beach Classic this April 9-12. This is NOT a tournament – it is an EXPERIENCE brought to you by PLAY AT THE PLATE BASEBALL. Looking for solid commitments from league ball players and only serious players need to inquire.
Here is the scoop from Scott Green – the Director of Play at the Plate Tournaments: The players will get the following: They will play 7 games over the 3 days (Doubleheaders first 3 days single game Sunday morning). A locker in the Cardinals Clubhouse, to safely store their gear and uniforms all weekend. Their uniforms will be washed and hung up after play on Thursday, Friday and Saturday. (We ask that the guys leave a tip for the clubbie) We provide all the towels, toiletries etc. A cold adult beverage in the clubhouse. A buffet lunch every day across the street at J.J. Muggs Sports Grille (Not Sunday). Access to the batting tunnels out by the fields. An early arrival BP session on Wednesday, with all the screens and pitching machine set up. 50/50 Raffles, gift raffles etc. A pro photographer who is able to sell photos to the players. A give away gift. A game inside Roger Dean Stadium.
For players looking to stay in the area we have a block of rooms set aside at the Embassy Suites in Palm Beach Gardens, one exit south of the Stadium. The hotel offers a complete full cooked to order breakfast and a free happy hour every evening. Add the free lunch from us and all they have to do is grab dinner at the multitude of restaurants in the area to fit any budget.
The usual price per player is $525. However, the SFBL will help sponsor the team so the player fee for the SFBL team is $200 per player. Are you game?
THE 12TH ANNUAL PALM BEACH CLASSIC — APRIL 9-12, 2015 — ROGER DEAN STADIUM — JUPITER, FLORIDA

Revised Player Ejection Policy:
Effective October 1, 2014, the league's player ejection policy have been modified as stated below.
Ejected Players: Any player ejected from an official league game for a minor offense will be tracked on the EJECTED PLAYERS LIST for the balance of the season. A second offense for a minor infraction during the same season will result in an automatic one game suspension. The slate is wiped clean after the season ends.
The chief umpire at the game will report whether the ejection was for a minor or major infraction. By way of example, ejections for arguing balls and strikes, throwing a bat or helmet or using foul language are minor infractions. Ejections for taunting, fighting, rough play or making contact with the umpire are examples of what would be deemed major infractions.
Pre-game and post game infractions will be enforced in the same fashion as in-game infractions. Confronting an umpire or another player in the parking lot after the game is an example of a post game infraction (in this case – a major infraction).
Ejected players guilty of a major offense will automatically be suspended for a minimum of one game. The League President will determine the length of any multi-game suspension in his sole discretion.
Suspended Players: A suspended player must sit out his team's next scheduled game following the suspension as well as any game thereafter until the suspension has been served. Suspended players will be tracked on the SUSPENDED PLAYERS LIST.
Any violation of the rule will result in the team forfeiting the game in which the suspended player was involved in.
Suspended players must pay a $100 reinstatement fee in order to become eligible to resume play. No exceptions. Repeat offenders are not welcome in the league.

The 2014 All Star Games took place January 11, 2015 at Cypress Bay – Vista Park in Weston, Florida. In the first game, Jay Warman of the South Florida Sting Rays (MVP) led the Senior Division All Stars to a decisive win over the Masters Division All Star squad. The second game, between the Premier Division All Stars and the Senior Division All Stars, was rained out.
The prior SFBL All Star Games were held at Flamingo Park in Miami Beach on Sunday, December 1, 2013, featuring two All Star games involving all teams. In Game 1 (9:00 am), the Senior Division All-Stars (with playoff teams Miami Blues, South Florida Reds, Perrine Monarchs, Dade Diamondbacks, Miami Brewers, Miami Black Bats) defeated the Premier Division All-Stars 11-1.
Game summary by Premier All-Stars manager Mickey Filippucci: The Senior Division A Team dominated 11-1. Kenny Tyson (Miami Blues) struck out 7 and allowed only one run over six innings, while the Senior team unleashed a hitting attack on Premier starter Alex Arteaga (Coral Gables A's). The Senior team scored 8 runs in the second inning. Yusbell Ortega (Dade Diamondbacks) led the attack (3-4, 2B, 2 RBI), followed by Bill Zoffinger (Miami Blues, 2-4, 2 RBI, 2B) and Donny Gagliano (SF Reds, 2-3, 2 RBI, 2 2B). For the Premier team, Brandon Stein (Miami Red Sox) and Eric Perez (Coral Gables A's) went 2-3.
Rosters included Premier Division managed by Miguel Garcia (Coral Gables A's, Miami Marlins, Miami Red Sox, Miami Dodgers, Miami Dbacks players such as Alex Arteaga, Leo Portes, Eric Perez, Brandon Stein) versus Senior Division managed by James Giordano (Perrine Monarchs, Dade Diamondbacks, Miami Black Bats, Miami Blues players such as Sean Dyer, Tony Guy, Yusbell Ortega, Michael Stalker, Kenny Tyson).

t: How to qualify for Spring Season Playoffs
b:
SFBL Playoffs:
With the second half of the season in full swing, it's time to talk about the playoff races. Also, it is vital to understand how a team qualifies for the post season action. Here is the scoop:
Premier Division:
Top four teams in the final regular season standings advance to the playoffs as follows:
Semi-finals: Game 1: #4 team versus #1 team; Game 2: #3 team versus #2 team.
Championship Series: The semi-finals winners advance to play a Best-of-Three Championship Series starting with a doubleheader on the first Sunday to be followed by a final game if necessary the subsequent Sunday.
Senior Division:
Top three teams in each Division in the final regular season standings advance to the playoffs as follows:
Quarter-finals: Broward #1 and Dade #1 teams receive a BYE; Game 1: Dade #3 team versus Broward #2 team; Game 2: Broward #3 team versus Dade #2 team.
Semi-finals: Game 3: Game 1 winner versus Broward #1 team; Game 4: Game 2 winner versus Dade #1 team.
Championship Series: The semi-finals winners advance to play a Best-of-Three Championship Series starting with a doubleheader on the first Sunday to be followed by a final game if necessary the subsequent Sunday.
Masters Division:
Top four teams in the final regular season standings advance to the playoffs as follows:
Semi-finals: Game 1: #4 team versus #1 team; Game 2: #3 team versus #2 team.
Championship Series: The semi-finals winners advance to play a Best-of-Three Championship Series starting with a doubleheader on the first Sunday to be followed by a final game if necessary the subsequent Sunday.

The Premier Division Coral Gables Athletics pulled off the three-peat by defeating the Miami Rays in the Premier Division Championship Series 2 games to 1 to claim their 11th title in the South Florida Baseball League.  With that title, the A’s further cemented their status as the winningest team in league history! What an accomplishment!  In the final game, the A’s unloaded on Rays pitching and won in a rout 13-3. They had previously come from behind to beat the Miami Red Sox 4-3 in the bottom of the 12th and the Rays knocked out the Perrine Monarchs 8-4 in the two semifinal games leading up to the championship series.
Meanwhile, in the Senior Division, the Southern Yankees survived a testy Miami Black Bats team, scoring two unearned runs in the bottom of the ninth to come from behind in a wild 14-13 contest to advance to the semifinals against top-seed Davie Tigers this coming Sunday October 19.  In the other quarterfinals game, the South Florida Beach Bums upset a very tough Miami Pirates club 4-0, after getting a 1-0 early lead and adding three runs late.  The Bums will now take on the veteran Miami Brewers in the second semifinal game for Senior Division this Sunday October 19.
Finally, the defending champions, Miramar Rangers, take on the former champions Palm Beach Angels in the Masters Division best of three Championship Series starting October 26. To get to the championship series, the Rangers blasted the South Florida Dodgers 17-11 and the Angels got by the Dade Nationals 9-3 in the Masters Division semifinals.

The SFBL is pleased to announce ALL STAR SUNDAY – January 11, 2015 to be held at CYPRESS BAY HIGH in Weston. In Game 1, the Masters Division All Stars will take on the Senior Division Team 2 All Stars, which is comprised of players from teams that did not make the playoffs last season. In Game 2, the Premier Division All Stars will square off against the Senior Division Team 1 All Stars, which is comprised of players from teams that made last season’s playoffs. After each game, the winning team will elect a Most Valuable Player. The two MVPs will receive their trophies on the field in the post game ceremony.
Team managers are asked to submit their player nominations to the league office by Wednesday, January 7, 2015. All Star team managers will be announced on Wednesday, January 7, 2015.

t: FALL 2016 BASEBALL BEAST – CORY HOPPER – Miami Red Sox
b:
Congratulations to Cory Hopper, our Fall 2016 Season BASEBALL BEAST!  Cory struck out 22 and pitched a complete game to lead the Miami Red Sox to a dramatic come from behind 9-8 win over the defending champions Davie Tigers.
Down by a run in the bottom of the eighth, the Red Sox had only 5 minutes to go before time would expire and prevent the team to get a final ninth inning chance.  So the Sox needed to get three outs in less than 5 minutes BUT Cory pulled it off, striking out two and getting a ground out on ten pitches! The Red Sox would go on to a two run rally and take the 9-8 lead in the ninth, and then Cory closed to the door, striking out the side to take his total to 22 strikeouts out of 27 outs in the ball game.  What a feat for the record books!!!  Cory Hopper – our Fall 2016 Season BASEBALL BEAST!
The SFBL tracks the best individual performance turned in each week via Player of the Week announcements. But every once in a while someone turns in an extraordinary performance. The “BASEBALL BEAST AWARD“ is awarded to the best individual performance of the season drawn from the list of Players of the Week winners. The SFBL conducts a special poll with the league team managers to determine the winner, who earns the BASEBALL BEAST trophy, an authentic SFBL logo baseball cap and permanent spot in the history books.

t: Fall 2013 Awards Banquet a Smash Hit
b:
The South Florida Baseball League Fall 2013 Awards Banquet was a rockin' event as the Miami Blues and the Dade Tigers took center stage to accept their awards and championship t-shirts Saturday night May 31, 2014 at Little Hoolies Sports Bar and Grill in Miami. The banquet featured rock band Head First and plenty of beer and wings.
The Miami Blues, captained by James Giordano, took home the Senior Division championship trophy while Will Dorsey of the Miami Blues took home the Premier MVP hardware. In the Masters Division, Rene Orovio and his Dade Tigers took the title, and Danger Villalobos of the Dade Tigers took home the Masters MVP trophy. The Coral Gables Athletics, led by Miguel Garcia, were unable to attend but will receive their Premier Division championship trophy at the next banquet. Carlos Alvarez of the Coral Gables Athletics earned the Premier Division MVP crown.
Rock band Head First lead singer (and former Miss Miami), Janae Deleon, presented Dade Tigers head coach Rene Orovio and Miami Blues skipper James Giordano with their Fall 2013 championship trophies. Billy Zoffinger, former MVP, accepted the MVP trophy on behalf of Premier Division MVP Will Dorsey of the Miami Blues, and Danger Villalobos of the Dade Tigers accepted the Masters Division MVP hardware. Congratulations to all of our newest champions!

The new Senior Division playoff structure is as follows:
Top three teams in Senior Division Dade bracket and Broward bracket qualify for post season baseball. The Dade County #1 seed and the Broward County #1 seed will have a bye for the 1st round of playoffs and will host the semi-finals, while the second and third place finishers play a quarterfinal game for the right to play the semi-final game against the top seeds. See tie-breaker rules where applicable. The Senior Division Finals consist of a best of three Championship Series, commencing with a doubleheader on the first Sunday of the Series.
Quarter-finals:
Game 1 – Broward County #3 seed (visitor) vs Dade County #2 seed (home); game will be played in Dade County.
Game 2 – Dade County #3 seed (visitor) vs Broward County #2 seed (home); game will be played in Broward County.
Semi-Finals:
Game 1 – Winner of Broward County #3 seed vs Dade County #2 seed (visitor) vs Broward County #1 seed (home); game will be played in Broward County.
Game 2 – Winner of Dade County #3 seed vs Broward County #2 seed (visitor) vs Dade County #1 seed (home); game will be played in Dade County.
Finals (best of three Championship Series): Winner of Semi-Finals game 1 vs Winner of Semi-Finals game 2. Team records will determine home / away and field location.

This page documents the banquet and awards sponsored by the South Florida Baseball League.
Awards Banquet – Championship Teams: The SFBL hosts an official awards banquet each year to honor the championship teams of each division. In addition to the fun and festivities of the "live event" banquet, the championship team managers are presented with a championship trophy.
League Most Valuable Player: A League MVP from each division receives an MVP trophy and special recognition at the awards banquet. The League MVP will be determined by a majority vote by a 5-man awards committee established by the League Office.
Leader Board Winners: As has been customary throughout the history of the SFBL, players who finish the season on top a Leader Board category (listed below) shall be recognized permanently in the history pages of the South Florida Baseball League web site.
Offensive Categories: Batting Champion, On-Base Percentage, Slugging Percentage, Base Hits, Runs Scored, Runs Batted In, Doubles, Triples, Home Runs, Stolen Bases.
Defensive Categories: Most Victories – Cy Young, Earned Run Average, Opponents Batting Average, Strikeouts, Saves.

t: Brewers and Beach Bums win Quarterfinals / play Semifinals vs White Sox and Sharks
b:
OPEN DIVISION SEMIFINALS
The Miami Brewers are no strangers to the playoffs having won a couple of titles in recent seasons. Well, they are back in the Open Division Semifinals by beating a talented South Florida Sting Rays 7-4 to take on the second seed Broward White Sox on June 4. The South Florida Beach Bums also made the final four and seek their second title. The Beach Bums, who took out the Miami Pirates in a hotly contested 5-2 win, now take on the top seed Weston Sharks in the second Open Division Semifinals game.
The South Florida Beach Bums - Weston Sharks semifinals game is scheduled for Sunday June 4 at 9:15 am at beautiful Flamingo Park on Miami Beach.
The Miami Brewers - Broward White Sox semifinals game is set for Sunday June 4 at 9:15 am at McArthur High in Hollywood.
The semifinals winners will play in the Open Division Championship on Sunday June 11.
In addition to carving out a permanent spot in the record book, the Champions win a beautiful championship trophy, an MVP trophy, and a $1000 cash bonus, championship t-shirts and authentic SFBL logo baseball caps for the players. Let the big dog eat!

Hit Zone Batting Cages presents Co-Players of the Week 9:
Cory Casuso – Miami Red Sox and Alex Arteaga – Coral Gables Athletics.  Both aces pitched a complete 10 inning shut out game against each other in the 0-0 tie game between the Sox and the A’s on July 20, 2014.  Can’t remember the last time that happened. What a game by both aces!
Cory Casuso
Alex Arteaga
As a side note, the Red Sox and the Athletics, which compete in the Premier Division, played earlier in the season,  The score of that one?  1-1 tie in 10 innings.  In the Believe It or Not category, Cory Casuso also went 10 innings in that one giving up just the one tying run in the bottom of the ninth inning before closing out the 10th.  Between the two games against arguably the best team in the State of Florida, Cory recorded two complete games, pitching 20 innings and giving up just the one run.
Cory and Alex both won a free hour at the batting cages courtesy of Hit Zone – Miami Tropical Park.
Hit Zone

Phone:  (305) 448-9663
Hours:  Daily 2:00 – 9:00 pm

The SFBL presents the 2016 All Star Game Sunday July 3 at Flamingo Park – Miami Beach. The contest will feature the traditional Dade vs Broward face-off, with each team in the league supplying two players. This year’s All Stars will receive authentic SFBL baseball caps! Stay tuned for further details!
Recapping the 2015 All Star Game: The South Florida Baseball League hosted the 2015 All Star Game on July 5, 2015 at the beautiful Flamingo Park on Miami Beach. Led by manager Dave Carnevale from the Davie Tigers, the American League Team beat the George Kenhan’s National League Team 11-7 in a come-from-behind effort. Last year’s All Star MVP Jay Warman, South Florida Sting Rays, recorded the biggest blast of the day with a 7th inning game-tying bomb to left field but teammate, Nestor Estevez, from the Broward Indians won this year’s All Star MVP with a performance that included going 2-3, with a double, triple, 3 RBI and clutch defense. Bobby Vega, Miami Brewers, and Alex Catala, South Florida Dodgers, each went 3-3 to lead the National Team in offense.

The South Florida Baseball League Playoffs are here! The League is wrapping up the regular season Sunday April 22 with a few select playoff spots on the line. The top three teams in each bracket qualify for playoffs in both the Masters and Open Divisions. The first place teams in each bracket receive a BYE while the third place teams visiting the second place teams in the Quarterfinals. The Quarterfinals winners then play the first place teams in the Semifinals. Finally, the winners of the Semifinals play a single game for the Championship in each Division.
The Championship teams take home a $1000 CASH PRIZE, custom SFBL logo caps and championship t-shirts for the players while the manager receives the team championship trophy. Finally, there is an MVP trophy to be awarded to the MVP on each championship team. Let the big dog eat!
Shown below are Coach Willie and his assistant getting the FLOMO Crusaders ready for the final battle against the Miami Pirates to make playoffs. Pregame sandwiches and Cuban coffee to get the team going!

SFBL PLAYOFF RULES – SPRING 2016 SEASON
OPEN DIVISION
Quarter-Finals: The first and second place teams earn a first round bye, while the 3 seed plays the 6 seed and the 4 seed plays the 5 seed in the quarter-finals.
Semi-Finals: The first place team plays the winner of the quarter-finals game between the 4 seed and the 5 seed. The second place team plays the winner of the quarter-finals game between the 3 seed and the 6 seed.
Championship: The semi-finals winners play a single championship game. In this winner-take-all contest the Champions earn a Championship Trophy, an MVP trophy and championship t-shirts along with a $1,000 CASH BONUS!!
MASTERS DIVISION
Semi-Finals: The first place team earns a bye. The second place team plays the third place team.
Championship: The semi-finals game winner plays the first place team in a single championship game. In this winner-take-all contest the Champions earn a Championship Trophy, an MVP trophy and championship t-shirts along with a $1,000 CASH BONUS!!
ROCK and ROLL my brothers!

The Fall 2013 Season Playoffs continue Sunday March 16, 2014. Leading off, the Masters Division Championship, which features a contest between between newcomer Dade Tigers (10-2) and the Palm Beach Angels (11-0-1).  Up second is the long-awaited Senior Division Semi-final game between two former champions, the Miami Brewers (7-6) and the Miami Blues (11-1).
The Davie Tigers (7-5) traveled south to take on the Perrine Monarchs (9-3) in a Quarter-final game in the Senior Division.
Finally, the Premier Division launches it playoffs with Semi-final games between the Miami Reds (3-2-1)and defending champs Coral Gables Athletics (9-3) in game 1 while game 2 features Miami Marlins (7-4-1) against the Miami Dodgers (8-4).
REMINDER – to be eligible for playoff participation, players MUST have played a minimum of 3 games during the regular season. Any team that uses an ineligible player will forfeit that playoff game. Managers, please check your rosters and make sure ALL of the players you will use are eligible for the playoffs.

t: Players of the Week 4 and Week 5 – George Kenhan & Brandon Sam – Miami Brewers
b:
Hit Zone Batting Cages presents Co-Players of the Week 4 and Week 5:
George Kenhan and Brandon Sam – Miami Brewers, threw a combined one hitter against the Miami Diamondbacks in the Brewers 9-0 win over the Miami Diamondbacks! Kenhan, the crafty veteran, and Sam, the swashbuckling leader of the new crop of ball players, were masterful, painting the corners all day long a la Rembrandt. They faced the bare minimum 18 batters in 6 innings in a game cut short by lightning – just a clean slate of great pitching. The DBacks had to deal with lightning from the sky and lightning off the mound – not fair!
Pitching machines they are  – George Kenhan and Brandon Sam, our Players of the Week 4 and Week 5!
Pictured above, Brandon Sam on the left and George Kenhan on the right – our Players of the Week 4 and Week 5!
George and Brandon each won a free hour at the batting cages courtesy of Hit Zone – Miami Tropical Park.
Hit Zone

Phone:  (305) 448-9663
Hours:  Daily 2:00 – 9:00 pm

The League Office would like to receive as many pictures as possible. From these pictures, a selection will be made and posted on the main page of the photo gallery. The other pictures will be featured on separate Photo Gallery pages that are designed for each team who submits pictures.
All pictures should be emailed as a jpeg or gif file to sfbl@hotmail.com. Please indicate who is in the picture (first and last name) along with the date and game played so that the picture can be properly identified.
The SFBL has only received some pictures for the Fall 2003 Season. Please send in your pictures to the email listed above to post new pictures.

The FWBL is hosting a PRO CAMP on Sunday June 19 and Monday June 20, 2016 at the beautiful Sabal Pines Park in Coconut Creek, Florida. The PRO CAMP runs from 10am – 2pm on Sunday June 19 and 10am – 2pm on Monday June 20, 2016. DAY 1 has drills designed to specifically improve tryout performance and DAY 2 is a PRO DAY featuring MLB and independent league scouts evaluating all players. The PRO CAMP registration fee is $199 per player for ONLINE registration or $220 for ON SITE registration. Prospects will receive a PRO CAMP T-Shirt and Baseball Cap and a chance to get scouted and signed to play independent league baseball.
Sabal Pines Park address is 5005 NW 39th Ave, Coconut Creek, FL 33073 – For more information call 305.496.2962.
The FWBL is an independent professional baseball league catering to College, Rookie and Single A level prospects. Florida Winter Baseball League – where tomorrow's stars play today!

t: SEASON RESUMES SEP 20, 2020 – SFBL VIRUS PROTOCOL
b:
Believe it or not, the season resumes this coming Sunday, September 20, 2020 with limited action as our baseball fields open back up.
New regulations. All players must adhere to the new Miami Dade County public parks mandate:
All players and coaches must wear a mask at all times. This includes players and umpires on the field of play and players and coaches in the dugouts.
No attendance permitted. No family, no friends, no fans. Only players and coaches permitted at Miami Dade County public parks.
Players and coaches must maintain the social distancing at all times to the extent possible.
All players and coaches must wash hands before and after games.
Any player or coach appearing sick or ill will be disqualified and must leave the facility immediately. Failure to do so will result in the game being suspended immediately.
Players must use their own helmets.
No spitting permitted while at the public parks.

t: Orlando Vipers Jump Out In Front at Miami Open
b:
The Orlando Vipers, skippered by veteran Jorge Munoz, looked impressive today posting two wins on the first day of the Miami Open tournament Saturday May 24, 2014. The Vipers pulled off a come-from-behind victory by scoring three runs in the bottom of the eighth to win 4-3 over the Aventura Braves. Then they dominated in a 10-0 shutout of the Miami Aqua Sox in game two at beautiful Flamingo Park on South Beach.
Meanwhile, the Aventura Braves rebounded to hold off a late Miami Red Sox rally to pick up a crticial 7-5 win. The Miami Red Sox had defeated the Miami Aqua Sox 7-4 in the early game at West Perrine Park.
Yet all four teams are still in the hunt to make the playoffs due to the round robin nature of the tournament. Tie breakers may have to be employed to advance the teams on Sunday May 25.
Tournament Standings after two rounds: (standings table not preserved in archived copy)

The South Florida Baseball League is pleased to present FALL 2018 Season to start Sunday December 9, 2018.  Team registration deadline is December 1, 2108.  This upcoming season, the SFBL will feature over 30 teams competing in three divisions; OPEN 18+, SENIOR 30+ and MASTERS 40+.  Individual player registration will remain open all season – players can join at any time during the season on a pro-rated basis.  The player registration fee remains at $230 per player.
This will be our 53rd consecutive season in our 26th year of operation!  We need our heads examined.
Winning teams in OPEN and SENIOR Divisions win a $1000 CASH PRIZE, championship t-shirts and custom authentic SFBL logo baseball caps for the players along with a Championship trophy for the manager and an MVP trophy for the horse that led them.  The MASTERS DIVISION top seed will compete in the SENIOR DIVISION playoffs.

t: Miami Bulldogs Win the 2012 Florida Cup Tournament
b:
The Miami Bulldogs, led by JP Fernandez, won their second consecutive Florida Hardball tournament, taking the 2012 Florida Cup title by crushing the Miami Red Sox 15-3 in the final held on Labor Day, September 3, 2012. The Dogs, who went undefeated in the tournament, had also won the 2012 Miami Open previously on Memorial Day. The Miami Red Sox have the distinction of having lost both finals to the Dogs.
The Dade Diamondbacks came into the tournament loaded but were taken out by the Sox in the semi-final game by a score of 6-4 in a game that went down to the last out. Meanwhile, the South Florida Rays were sent home early after lackluster elimination round play.
Congratulations to the Miami Bulldogs for a job well done! They walked off the field with the tournament trophy, championship t-shirts and a whole lot of pride.

t: Welcome To South Florida Baseball League
b:
About SFBL
The South Florida Baseball League (SFBL) is one of the premier adult baseball organizations in the great state of Florida operating in Dade, Broward and Palm Beach counties. The SFBL caters to adult men of all ages who crave baseball, offering competition in three divisions; Open 18+, Senior 30+ and Masters 40+. The SFBL is a WOOD BAT LEAGUE. Considered by many to be one of the top adult amateur hardball organizations in the country, the SFBL provides high quality regional baseball competition in South Florida, including league play, all star games and tournaments.
Player Registration: WE ARE BASEBALL - South Florida style - beautiful weather, 3 age brackets 18+, 30+, 40+, wood bat, Minor League baseballs, high quality fields and umpires and year-round baseball - only in South Florida - baseball heaven!
