t: Miami Aqua Sox looking for Tournament Players
b:
The Miami Aqua Sox tournament team is competing in the upcoming 2014 Miami Open Baseball Tournament and is looking for interested tournament players – all positions open. As you know, the SFBL always enters a team into each tournament consisting of players from around the league. Sometimes the Sox do great, other times not so great but at least the participating players got a chance to experience tournament ball.
The player registration fee is $60, which guarantees you three games minimum, five games maximum if the team reaches the finals.
The team will limit the player count to 15 players so first 15 players that pay are in. Use the Player Registration and Pay Online options on this website to register for the team. Come join the team and experience the next level of competitive baseball. Are you game?

t: Miami Brewers Win Another Open 18 PLUS Division Title 10-6 over Delray Nationals
b:
Miami Brewers Win Another Open 18 PLUS Division Title 10-6 over Delray Nationals
Photo caption: Brewers Skipper, George Kenhan (left), and Sean Hernandez, MVP, enjoy another big time win!
After a brief hiatus, the Miami Brewers proved their durability by returning to the OPEN 18 PLUS Division Finals Sunday, December 8, 2019, at Plantation Sunset Park to match up with a tough Delray Nationals team. And, yes, the Brewskies won another title, capitalizing on uncharacteristic sloppy defense, and blasting the Nationals 10-6. Brewers ace, Sean Hernandez, won the MVP honors, pitching a complete game and leading the Brew Crew all season. Delray Nationals catcher and team manager, Ryan Snyder, hit a long bomb in the losing effort to highlight the Nats offense.

The SFBL presents the Masters Division Semifinals this coming Sunday May 1, 2016.
In Game 1, the four seed Southern Yankees take on the two-time defending champions and heavy favorite, Palm Beach Angels at 10 am at Stoneman Douglas Park in Parkland. In Game 2, the three seed Miramar Rangers take on the two seed Miami Yankees at 9:00am at McArthur High. The semifinals winners advance to the Masters Division Championship game Sunday May 15, where the winning team earns the Championship Trophy and a $1000 cash bonus along with Championship t-shirts and a spot in the record books! May the Baseball Gods smile upon you! Rock n Roll.
Pictured: Miguel Adrian, Miramar Rangers, and Ray Asmar, Southern Yankees skipper contemplating his next move.

t: Ft Lauderdale Pilots Take MASTERS 40 PLUS Title with 11-0 Win vs Miami Cardinals
b:
The Fort Lauderdale Pilots won the Spring 2019 Masters 40 Plus Division Championship with an 11-0 win over the surprising Miami Cardinals on Sunday, November 24, 2019 at Northeast High Baseball Park. Mike Fields was named Most Valuable Player, and team manager Jamie Siragusa collected another trophy for his substantial collection.
Documenting the incredible run by the Fort Lauderdale Pilots and the Ft. Lauderdale Angels (when the team played in the Senior 30 Plus Division): 9 championships with a cumulative all-time record of 166-7-3, including 107 wins out of the last 108 games, an 80-game winning streak and a current 27-game winning streak. Photos featured Mike Fields (MVP), Mike Fields with Jamie Siragusa, and Alvaro Gomez.

The South Florida Beach Bums (#4 seed) will battle the top seeded Weston Sharks while the Davie Tigers (#3 seed) slug it out with the South Florida Sting Rays (#2 seed) in the OPEN DIVISION Broward Bracket.
The OPEN DIVISION quarterfinals winners play in the semifinals on May 1 and the Championship after Mother's Day on May 15.
The MASTERS DIVISION playoffs begin May 1, 2015. ROCK ON!

t: Miami Red Sox announce 2014 Florida Cup commitment
b: The Miami Red Sox announced yesterday, June 9, 2014, that the team has officially entered the upcoming 2014 Florida Cup, which will be held in Miami over the Labor Day Weekend at the end of August. The Red Sox have a long history of tournament ball, having traveled and competed all over the State of Florida over the past two decades. The team has one championship (2004 Miami Open) and numerous runner-up trophies from Disney Classic to the Fall Classic and other MSBL and Florida Hardball tournaments over the years. The Sox are on a mission to win it all so look for a feisty team to take the field in this year's Florida Cup!

t: Orlando Madness and Naples Marlins coming to Florida Cup Baseball Tournament
b:
The Orlando Madness from the Central Florida Baseball League and the Naples Marlins from the Fort Myers Baseball League have recently expressed interest in participating in the upcoming Florida Cup Baseball Tournament over the Labor Day Weekend in Miami – Miami Beach (August 30 – September 1). The wood bat only tournament is open to all teams from any league with the age limit of 18 years and older.
They join the Miami Red Sox, Miami Aqua Sox and the Beck Express in teams participating in the tournament.
Tournament Field:
1. Miami Red Sox
2. Miami Aqua Sox
3. Orlando Madness
4. Beck Express Miami
5. Naples Marlins

The SFBL presents the Spring 2017 Open Division Quarterfinals this coming Sunday December 3, 2017. Game 1 features the American League third seed FM Crusaders against the second seed Miami Brewers at 9 am at Florida Memorial University. In the second Quarterfinals, the National League third seed, South Florida Beach Bums, match up against the second seed Broward White Sox at 12:30 pm at Florida Memorial University. The National League top seed, Davie Tigers, have a BYE and will play the winner in the Semifinals.

July 22, 2018 - Kendall Metz rode a horse named Andrew Smiley all the way to the MASTERS CHAMPIONSHIP, beating the Palm Beach Angels 8-6 in a come-from-behind sneak attack. Congratulations - the Metz really earned it. Also, special mention to the Palm Beach Angels, who had won the previous 7 titles in a row in the most amazing run in SFBL history. The Kendall Metz collected all the SFBL Hardware, winning the Masters Championship trophy, the MVP trophy and Andrew Smiley won the Baseball Beast Award. The team also won the $1000 cash prize, championship t-shirts and custom SFBL logo caps for the players!

Congratulations to Jay Warman of the South Florida Sting Rays, who was this Season's All Star Game MVP. Fending off a strong challenge by Howard Capote-Miami Cardinals (monster solo home run), Juan Machado-Deerfield White Sox (3-3) and Richard Perez-Miami Dodgers (2-3, 2 RBI), Jay put his team ahead by getting 3 RBI in his first two at bats (2-3, 3 RBI) and the Senior Division All Stars cruised to a 9-3 win over the Masters Division All Stars in a much closer-than-the-score game. Picture above is of Seniors All Star coach, Manny Fernandez - Deerfield White Sox with All Star MVP Jay Warman. GO Jay!

The SFBL presents the Open Division Playoffs – Quarterfinals – Sunday May 21. The 3rd Seed Miami Brewers take on the South Florida Sting Rays while the 4th Seed South Florida Beach Bums square off against the Miami Pirates in the Quarterfinals at Miami Beach Flamingo Park. Top seed, Weston Sharks, and 2nd Seed, Broward White Sox, earned a bye and will take on the winners in the Semifinals June 4.
The champions earn a team championship trophy, an MVP trophy, a $1000 cash bonus, championship tshirts and authentic SFBL logo caps for the players, and, of course, a permanent spot in the record books!

t: Fall 2020 Masters Division Semifinals Game booked for Sunday June 6 – Dade Nationals v Southern Yankees
b:
The SFBL presents the Fall 2020 Season Masters Division Semifinals contest this coming Sunday, June 6, between the defending Champions, Dade Nationals, and the great pinstriped Southern Yankees. Skipper Ray Asmar and the Southern Yankees look to win the big one BUT they must first get by the defending champions, Dade Nationals, in the Semifinals. PRESSURE COOKER SUNDAY!
Awaiting the winner in the Finals are the Miami Cardinals, last season’s runner-up.
The winning team earns a Championship Trophy, an MVP trophy, championship t-shirts and authentic SFBL logo caps, and a $500 cash prize. Nice!

t: All Broward Chiropractic and Rehab is a proud sponsor of SFBL
b: All Broward Chiropractic and Rehab has teamed up with the South Florida Baseball League to bring its state-of-the-art facility and highest quality chiropractic care to the league. Founded by SFBL veteran player Dr. Mike Surdis, the practice is located in Pembroke Pines, in the heart of SFBL country. Mention SFBL and receive a 10% discount on your first paid consultation. The doctors have treated SFBL players and other South Florida sports leagues for years. For an appointment, call 954.443-2420. Located at 1551 North Palm Avenue, Pembroke Pines, FL 33026. Visit www.drsurdis.com for details.

The SFBL proudly announces Opening Day for the Spring 2014 – April 27, 2014. ROCK n ROLL.
This will be our 23rd year and 45th season in a row – hard to believe, but true nonetheless. We love baseball.
New players and new teams are welcome to join and compete – just register online and we will hook you up playing HARDBALL!
This season we will be rolling out the updated History Book as well as the HALL OF FAME. Also, Florida Hardball presents the Miami Open Baseball Tournament at the end of May, 2014 as well as the Florida Cup Baseball Tournament Labor Day – early September. PLAY BALL!

As everyone knows, baseball is a sport based on stats and records. Therefore, the SFBL has designed this page to showcase the records of the league.
There are two individual record books: a Team Record Book and an Individual Player Record Book. The Team Record Book will feature such categories as most games played, most wins, most losses, most championships, most playoff appearances, etc. The Individual Player Record will feature all the Leaderboard categories as well as other records that have been collected in the long SFBL history.
Sections: Player Record Book, Team Record Book.

t: Opening Day Spring 2015 Season set for May 17, 2015
b:
South Florida Baseball League is pleased to announce OPENING DAY SPRING 2015 SEASON to be held Sunday May 17, 2015. This will be our 24th year and 46th season in a row – hard to believe, but true nonetheless. We love baseball. This season includes the 2015 All Stars Games and the Florida Cup Baseball Tournament (Labor Day Weekend). Team managers are encouraged to contact the league office to schedule preseason games. PLAY BALL!
New players and new teams are welcome to join. Player Registration is open 24/7. Call the league office at 305.496.2962 or register online at www.sfbl.com.

The Hall of Fame will be a page dedicated to those who have played and play
an instrumental part in the South Florida Baseball League.  Managers,
players, umpires and others will be honored for their contributions to the
League or their outstanding performance during play on Sundays.  Each Hall
of Fame member will have their picture and a short biography featured on this
page.
If you have any input on who should be among the first inducted into the SFBL
Hall of Fame, you can contact the league office by clicking here.
The inaugural group will announced shortly.  Check back soon!

Florida Rock Stars presents the Palmetto Bay Food and Music Festival, Veteran’s Day, November 11, 2012 at the Palmetto Bay Village Center located at 18001 Old Cutler Road, Miami, FL 33157.
This event is dedicated to honoring our Veterans and will feature a special awards ceremony in conjunction with Thinking of You Services, a veterans support organization that provides care packages to our troops overseas!
SFBL players and a guest are invited to attend this event FREE OF CHARGE. Password for complimentary cover charge is “SFBL”.
Click here for more information on this event.

t: FWBL Tryouts – Pompano Sports Complex Saturday September 19 – 9:30 AM
b:
The Florida Winter Baseball League is pleased to announce that it is holding its first FWBL Tryouts this coming Saturday, September 19, 2015 at the Pompano Baseball Complex in Pompano Beach. Tryouts begin at 9:30am. Interested players can register online at a fee of $50. Cash will be accepted at the Tryouts. Players will be scouted by league representatives from the four league teams. The Florida Winter Baseball League will commence the 30 game 2015 Season on October 16, 2015 at the Pompano Baseball Complex.
Florida Winter Baseball League – where tomorrow's stars play today!

t: Masters Division Semifinals set for June 11
b:
The South Florida Baseball League presents the Masters Division Semifinals Sunday June 11.
Game 1 features five-time defending champions and top seed, Palm Beach Angels, who take on the fourth seed Southern Yankees. The pin-striped ones are dreaming of an upset. The contest will take place at 9:30 am on June 11 at the gorgeous Sabal Pines Park in Coconut Creek.
The second Semifinals game features former champions and second seed, Miramar Rangers and the upstart third seed, South Florida Dodgers. The game is scheduled for June 11 at 9:15 am at Florida Memorial University.

The SFBL recognizes the one (and only) Ray “Raynman” Asmar as a special friend of the league and one of the longest tenured managers the league has ever had. Gentleman Raynman manages the Southern Yankees and has always had a knack for making his Monday morning story line an interesting read. His color commentary is legendary and an inspiration to all SFBL league members and his band of Southern Yankees are always on the hunt. Looking forward to seeing the Southern Yankees on the field in their return for another season!

t: Miami Brewers open Fall 2016 Season against Hialeah Astros December 18, 2016
b:
The SFBL is pleased to announce that Opening Day for Fall Season is December 18, 2016. The League will be welcoming several new teams in both the Open and Masters Divisions. This will be our 49th Consecutive Season in a row. Rock and Roll!
The opening game features former League Champions, Miami Brewers, against newcomers, Hialeah Angels at Miami Christian School Sunday December 18, 2016. The regular season for the rest of the league commences January 15, 2017 and runs through March 2017. More details to follow.

t: Beach Bums, Crusaders, Indians and Mariners win in Quarterfinals
b:
In a wild weekend of Playoffs, the SFBL Quarterfinals took place June 4, 2018.
Recapping the Quarterfinals:
Open Division Quarterfinals
Game 1: South Florida Beach Bums beat the Weston Leones 6-5 in a nail biter
Game 2: Flomo Crusaders upset the Miami Pirates by a score of 7-1
Masters Division Quarterfinals
Game 1: Miami Mariners shut out the Miami Reds 6-0 in dominating fashion
Game 2: Broward Indians surprised the Miramar Rangers 8-3
Shown below – Michael Jordan – South Florida Beach Bums

t: Masters Division Semifinals / Nationals-Angels and Reds-Rangers – JAN 7
b:
The SFBL presents the Masters Division Semifinals Sunday January 7, 2018. The Dade Nationals take on the six time defending champions, Palm Beach Angels in Game 1 and the Miramar Rangers hosting the Miami Reds in Game 2. The winners play in Masters Division Finals to be held January 14, 2018.
The winning teams in each division earn a Championship Trophy, an MVP Trophy, and championship t-shirts, authentic SFBL logo baseball caps and a $1,000 CASH PRIZE for the players! Let the big dog eat!

t: Masters Division Semifinals Dodgers vs Rangers Jun 25
b: The SFBL presents the Masters Division Semifinals Sunday June 25, 2017. Having survived the Southern Yankees 15-14 in the first semifinals matchup, the top seed and five time defending champions, Palm Beach Angels, are undefeated and looking to win another title. Standing in their way would be the winner of the second semifinals game between the Miramar Rangers and the South Florida Dodgers. The Dodgers take on the Rangers at 9:00 am June 25 at Florida Memorial University. Let’s rumble!

t: SFBL Managers Meetings Set for Saturday MAY 30, 2015
b: The SFBL Managers Meetings for Spring 2015 Season is set for this coming Saturday May 30, 2015. The Broward Managers Meeting will be held at the Hooters in Pembroke Pines at noon. The Dade Managers Meeting will be held at the Buffalo Wild Wings at 3pm. Agenda: Wings, Beer and Baseball. We will be distributing baseballs, score books and reviewing new season information. If a manager is unable to attend, please contact the league office to make arrangements to send an assistant manager.

t: OPENING DAY for Spring Season reset for May 31!
b: OPENING DAY for Spring 2015 Season has been reset for May 31, 2015. South Florida Baseball League is pleased to announce that this will be our 23rd year and 46th season in a row – hard to believe, but true nonetheless. We love baseball. This season includes the 2015 All Stars Games and the Miami Open (Memorial Day Weekend) and Florida Cup baseball tournaments (Labor Day Weekend). Team managers are encouraged to contact the league office to schedule preseason games. PLAY BALL!

t: Players of the Week 10 – Bryan Ocana and Andy Perez – Miami Amigos
b: South Florida Baseball League presents Players of the Week 10 – Bryan Ocana and Andy Perez – Miami Amigos, who threw a combined no-hitter against the feisty Kendall Metz. Nobody throws no hitters against the Kendall Metz but somehow the historic event happened November 10, 2019 at the beautiful Florida Memorial University Baseball Park. Miami Amigos always pitching up a storm – Bryan Ocana and Andy Perez very impressive with the NO NO – our Players of the Week 10!

t: Player of the Week 12 – Rodney Riera – Valma Senators
b: South Florida Baseball League presents Players of the Week 12 – Rodney Riera – Valma Senators, who pitched a monster complete game to lead his Senators to a 6-1 win over the fierce Miami Amigos in the Senior Division Championship on December 8, 2019 at the beautiful Florida Memorial University Baseball Park. Rodney was dominant all day, striking out 12 hitters and limiting the Amigos to three hits. Mr. Clutch when the chips were down. What a class performance!

t: New SFBL Rules Committee members appointed
b:
The SFBL announces the formation of the SFBL Rules Committee effective Spring 2013 season.
The inaugural members of the committee are: Felipe Florentino, Dave Carnevale, and Ernie Jorge.
Responsibilities include: 1. Update rules 2. Maintain and monitor official rosters 3. Enforcement of ejections 4. Protest resolution
**Please note – managers will deal directly with the league commissioner on rules enforcement and will not contact committee members directly.

t: Miami Aqua Sox looking for players for South Beach Slam Tournament
b:
The Miami Aqua Sox are an SFBL tournament all-star team.
The Aqua Sox have had great success in past tournaments and have a formidable roster for this year’s South Beach Slam Tournament.
Any SFBL players interested in playing with The Aqua Sox please contact Mickey at: telephone – (305) 496-2962, email – playball@sfbl.com.
Entrance fee is $60 per player. Minimum 3 games guaranteed + playoffs. There are still several positions available, including pitchers.

t: Masters Finals – South Florida Dodgers vs Dade Nationals
b: The SPRING 2021 Season Masters Division Finals take place this Sunday December 5. The game, to be held at Miami Christian School Ball Park, features the rejuvenated South Florida Dodgers challenging the defending champions, Dade Nationals. It’s been a while since the Dodgers have tasted post season action so the Nationals are not taking them for granted, even though they dominated both regular season games between the two teams. Let the big dog eat!

t: Lexis 24 a proud sponsor of the 2014 Miami Open Tournament
b: Lexis 24 is a proud sponsor of the 2014 Miami Open Baseball Tournament. The South Florida Baseball League will be using the Lexis 24 Sport baseballs exclusively for the tournament. These raised seam gems are the real deal, manufactured to Pro specifications, and already used by numerous independent, winter and Venezuelan baseball leagues. Visit lexisport24.blogspot.com for the full story or contact Javier Useche at 305.777.3528 to place an order.

t: Angels want FOUR-PEAT versus Miami Yankees
b:
The three-time defending Palm Beach Angels take on the surging Miami Yankees in the Masters Division Championship to be held Sunday May 15, 2016 at Sunset Park in Plantation. The Yankees knocked off perennial power Miramar Rangers, while the Angels survived the Southern Yankees 12-11 in the two semifinal games. Let the big dog eat!
Shown below are Luis Cancino, Manager of the Miami Yankees, and Jamie Siragusa, Manager of the Palm Beach Angels.

The Palm Beach Angels repeated as Masters Division Champions, squeezing by the Miramar Rangers 5-4 on Jamie Siragusa’s heroic two out RBI single in the bottom of the ninth. Not to be outdone, the Miami Brewers rode a horse named Sean Hernandez to a 4-1 victory over the Davie Tigers to repeat as Open Divisions Champions. The winning teams each earned the $1,000 cash prize! Congratulations to the Angels and Brewers – the best teams in the SFBL!

t: Fall 2020 Season Open Division Semifinals – Sunday June 6 – Nationals v Cubs and Brewers v FLOMO
b: The SFBL presents the 2020 Fall Season OPEN DIVISION Semifinals this coming Sunday, June 6 in the march to determine which team can be considered among the best in all of South Florida! First up, the power house Delray Nationals take on the former champions, South Florida Cubs. In the second contest, four time champions, Miami Brewers, look to win again against last season's champions, FLOMO Crusaders. Both games will be explosive!

The South Florida Baseball League has hired Jay Habacht as Media Director. Jay has been updating the SFBL website and will be adding new features, such as weekly videos and pictures. Managers are encouraged to send Jay your best pictures and videos of your games each week. Please email Jay at jay@sfbl.com or he can be reached at (305) 439-2644. Jay will also be building the SFBL brand through Twitter, Facebook and other outlets.

t: Miami Marlins sweep Boca Red Sox to win Premier / Miramar Rangers win Masters
b:
The Miami Marlins swept the Boca Red Sox 3-1 and 9-2 in the Premier Division championship series to take their second consecutive Premier Division title.  Not to be outdone, the Miramar Rangers got by the Hollywood Twins 10-4 to also win their second consecutive Masters Division title.
The Senior Division championship takes place this Sunday March 24 pitting the upstart Dade Diamondbacks against the former champions Miami Blues.

t: Opening Day June 24 ROCK AND ROLL
b:
The South Florida Baseball League is pleased to announce that OPENING DAY for our new Spring 2018 Season is June 24, 2018.
This will be our 52nd season in a row going all the way back to the Spring of 1992.
The SFBL is introducing a brand new 40+ Division this coming season. Teams will now compete in one of three age divisions, 18+, 30+ and 40+.
The SFBL Managers Meetings will take place Saturday afternoon June 23, 2018.

t: Fall 2015 Season BASEBALL BEAST Award - Zac Miller - South Florida Sting Rays
b: The SFBL presents our Fall 2015 Season BASEBALL BEAST Award to Zac Miller of the South Florida Sting Rays. Zac puts on an offensive show each game, and on one Sunday during the Sting Rays' championship season he racked up 7 RBI, going 4-4 with a 3-run home run to power his team to the greater glory. Zac earned a huge BEAST Trophy, an authentic SFBL logo cap and T-Shirt, and a special place in the league history books.

South Florida Baseball League presents Opening Day for Fall Season on Sunday, November 15, 2015. The SFBL will run two divisions 18+ and 30+ with the championship teams of each division winning the $1,000 CASH BONUS along with the usual hardware. New players and new teams are encouraged to contact the league office at 305.496.2962. Interested players need to register and pay $230 fee online at www.sfbl.com. ROCK ON!

t: SFBL vs Federal League Inter-League All Star Game Dec 29 2019
b: The SFBL is pleased to announce that it has accepted a challenge from the Federal League to compete in a year end Inter-League All Star Game. The contest, which will feature some of the best ball players in all of South Florida, is scheduled to take place Sunday, December 29, 2019 at 10 am at the Northeast High Baseball Complex in Fort Lauderdale. Stay tuned for more details to follow. Let the big dog eat!

Florida Rock Stars is looking for BARTENDERS for its upcoming South Dade Food and Country Festival on Saturday October 27, 2012. The festival will be held at Harris Field Pavilion located at 1034 NE 8th Street, Homestead, FL 33030.
Experience not required. Will train.

The SFBL will hold its pre-season Managers Meetings this coming Saturday JAN 5 with the Broward/Palm Beach team meeting taking place at 12:00 noon at the Hooters in Pembroke Pines at Pines and University and the Dade team meeting taking place at 3:00 pm at the Carolina Ale House on Bird Road and 67th Ave. Please make arrangements to be there to pick up your baseballs and scorebook.

t: Catcher Wanted for MSBL Tournament in Arizona
b:

For more information, please call 786-295-8989

t: South Florida Reds looking for new players
b: The South Florida Baseball League is pleased to announce the signing of Donny Gagliano as manager of the new expansion team South Florida Reds. The Reds will compete in the Senior Division and have a number of positions available. Interested players should contact Donny as soon as possible at 954-655-5546.

t: Brickell Orioles looking for new players
b: The Brickell Orioles are a new team coming in to The South Florida Baseball League. The Orioles will be competing in the Senior Division this season. There are several different positions currently available. Interested players please contact Mickey Filippucci at (305) 496-2962 or email playball@sfbl.com.

t: Fall Classic Team looking for a quality pitcher – fee covered
b: Team playing in Fall Classic in Clearwater, Nov 2-7, 35 division , constant playoff appearances, couple close losses in Finals, NEED A QUALITY PITCHER or two more to get it done this year! Pitchers will have their tournament fees paid! Please email if interested or need info at coreyapple@aol.com.

t: Miami Red Sox to enter 2015 Miami Open
b: The Miami Red Sox have officially signed on to compete in the upcoming 2015 Miami Open to be held at Flamingo Park, Miami Beach over the Memorial Day Holiday Weekend, May 23-25, 2015. It's been way too many years since the Sox last won a tournament so the Sox are gunning to win it all this time!

The Men's Senior Baseball League (MSBL) is the largest adult baseball organization in the world. The SFBL has affiliated with the MSBL since its inception in 1992. The MSBL offers the best in national adult baseball tournaments. For more information on MSBL, visit www.msblnational.com.

t: THE DEERFIELD WHITE SOX ARE LOOKING FOR PLAYERS
b: The Deerfield White Sox are looking for ballplayers 18 and over to compete this Fall Season 2012. The White Sox are scheduled to start on 11/4/12. Interested players from the greater Miami and Fort Lauderdale area should contact Andy Botett @ 305-712-0334 to be placed on the team.

t: Father’s Day break – all games postponed
